[{"Name":"Szabó Niki","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV84fSuMdL2CvUMYb8KTIx8o8N_acVCSyv6Jhzs_qi8PGzXEFft=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Too expensive for what it is. There’s only 2 room, and we thought there will be more to see. There was only a few people inside and wasn’t any line at the entrance.
I really liked the horror part though, that was pretty cool.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB_ebNOiQ5DETbdlRgz2VtP&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-22"},{"Name":"Elona Mendelovich","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXAv8jvh5kGg_s9ACrPeqXHq8uDb5qWUTkl7ZQw69QCYwrK5-qNUw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The place is well-designed and nice with unique themes. A tourist has a little difficulty understanding some of the displays or characters, but I'm sure locals understand everything. Horror movie lovers have an impressive portion of the display.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDEnRRUHgJ1T6epDxqx-1OE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2025-10-16"},{"Name":"Ray Recce","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVaLUzLvo5cjXZS1ZzCMxys31WGA7W0ybQPm3fN8OEkc5vtYjM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We had a nice visit with family, we specially liked the horror part. A lot of famous people to see and the train was also a nice experience. We bought the tickets online but there was no queue at the museum.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7jsjMdA&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-15"},{"Name":"Mariangela de Gemmis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXdSMyx0obDpTCrQyAZqsYS8HR7RH0Kbw6FCjqH_fUIMVrn6l0OPQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very nice experience, would recommend. In the ticket are included 3 "galleries" to visit, a small fun horror area upstairs and a "train of terror" ride, all inside of that building! I was surprised by how big in reality it is, they planned it very well.

The statues are incredibly detailed but some of them feel cramped and out of place? (Like Leonardo Di Caprio's statue in a modern suit in the Western area). But overall I loved the museum!

They also have an AR experience on their website which you can activate through the QR codes! They give additional information about the characters in display and activities that make you temporarely unlock filters for instagram and tiktok, it's very cute. Only flaw is that the website was very slow and because of that it took a lot of time for me to visit the exposition... almost 3 hrs! But you will take way less if you speed up or ignore the QR codes I guess. Your choice!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDbr93UBQ&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-8-10"},{"Name":"Vivo Vázquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvUyRyXlZBRb-TT4BhrnwZ8T78FNkcDjPnfNdsrNdFZg9teg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de Cera in Madrid is a fun and entertaining place for people of all ages. The museum showcases a wide variety of wax figures, including both Spanish and international celebrities, famous athletes, and historical personalities. The dedicated horror section adds a unique touch of excitement. Staff are helpful, and the experience is well organized, with easy access and no long queues. If you enjoy exploring cultural attractions or taking creative photos, this museum is an enjoyable stop during your visit to Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cherry Blossom","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVzTc_9PiI119Xy0q0bT8F1EkxZ5bBoeD_190WYbA3Wn9pk_BJ_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Only some of the attendants are helpful, the others would just stare at you instead of showing you the way to go (not marked otherwise).
The museum has got a majority of Spanish personalities/ celebrities. It's got a dedicated horror upstairs, although it all feels a bit creepy. 😄","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Chryso Konstantinou","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVak3LGZKu-DY7RPzeR_-fFfSuk4zZ5ICrZySNLg5dSR4y4d1cZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"We visited the Wax Museum and were very disappointed with the top floor experience. It is supposed to be entertaining and fun, not something that scares visitors away. The staff member upstairs yesterday really frightened us — he chased us almost to the very end. I was with my 7-year-old son, and in the end we had to turn back and leave because we were both so terrified that it was no longer enjoyable.

There should be limits to this kind of performance. It’s fine to create a spooky atmosphere, but not to pursue visitors all the way through. That only ruins the experience and achieves the opposite of what it should.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Johnny Rodriguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWiul4NlavDOHRZ5rs97Ih05Ja0sL6bIWBFTlxJECvl0fKzmRGx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"During my recent visit to the wax museum in Madrid, I had the pleasure of being guided by Yini, an outstanding tour guide who truly enhanced my experience. Yini's knowledge and enthusiasm brought each wax figure to life, making the tour both educational and entertaining. Her engaging storytelling and passion for the museum's exhibits made the visit truly memorable. I highly recommend taking a tour with Yini for a fantastic and insightful journey through the world of wax figures. Thank you, Yini, for your wonderful guidance and for making my visit to the museum so special!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICr_YfezAE&fid=0x0:0xb6063c8e78ceb647"],"When":"2024-7-10"}]

[{"Name":"Madeleine Dieleman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXVFk4NupipoXLc_9SNsxAKpEIJSwWCFtnLYo0zADDh-j2O5HF_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is the spot if you like modern art or ceramics and old gentlemen house interior. It’s a perfect thing to do if you want a mix of history and new conceptual art. There was also a good collection of Bauhaus and some famous names likes Behrens, rietveld, etc. Dont miss this if you like musea and interior","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA4vnc7EHYTjbwR68oPe9Vs&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2026-1-23"},{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is another of Madrid‘s fabulous museums. Set in over 60 rooms and several floors this museum, displays paintings, pottery, glassware, silverware furniture, and other items found in well to do family homes. Though the 17th and 18th centuries are featured on the ground floor there are temporary exhibitions, which include Contemporary works. Such a wonderful place and free entry. It’s highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA_wwQhTlcwVWpc6N4_5qkw&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-9-25"},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The pieces they have are great, but there are very few labels or descriptions of what the items are. For example, there are drawers in the textile room that you can open with beautiful pieces of textiles -- and not a single label. There's a small room with jewelry and other items, mostly clerical items, again, not a single label. The only things that are labeled are the larger pieces in glass cases, but even those are lacking in information. The temporary exhibits are usually much better explained. I really want to love this museum, as they do have wonderful pieces, but it appears that it is mostly designed for guided tours.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCDSMMlHLLlq7sS-lX-2nRe&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2026-1-14"},{"Name":"NYS Counselor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLwuzsNPG9_q0me8YL3lQpOMkuA9iChayY37Fr-rJdc4F3Jcw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The National Museum of Decorative Arts is a trip back in time, not only for the content of the displays, but for the way in which it is setup, which sometimes sadly reflects museum practice of an earlier era and no doubt a lack of funding to support upgrading the installations. While this is definitely not a museum designed for busloads of tourists, for those who love the decorative arts and have some knowledge of their history it is well worth a visit, particularly as it is very conveniently located only a short walk from the painting galleries of the famed Prado Museum.
We were there on a warm day which emphasized that the museum lacks air conditioning, presenting a hazard not only for the objects, but for staff and visitors as well. We were originally told that high temperatures did not allow opening the top floor, then, while were on the floor below, a staff member came over and graciously informed us there the top floor had been reopened on a very temporary basis. Fortunately, we moved with dispatch, as we had barely completed a circuit of the exhibitions upstairs before the heat forced it to be shut down again.
There are many fascinating pieces, some in vitrines, some in room-like displays, about which we would very much have liked to know more, as labels were minimal where they existed and largely of course in Spanish. Several fascinating pieces of furniture deserved far greater documentation, as did excellent displays of ceramics representing many centuries of Spanish skill and creativity and reflecting multiple different cultural influences. Noteworthy was a fine special exhibition devoted to glass, as well as a standing display of an historic kitchen featuring beautiful tile-work.
Although there were a handful of books on display in a case at the admissions desk, there is no gift shop and it isn't possible to preview their publications to determine if they may be of interest for purchase. In addition to historical displays, the Museum features changing special exhibitions, including while we were there one on fashion photography! Despite its limitations as a building, if time allows we will try to return on a cooler day to better explore the displays. There are certainly many fine works here that can't be found elsewhere, deserve more time, and will repay the attention of careful viewers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCk6SYwZQS0c9NrejYsJFBz&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Maria Deschamps","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVsIezNK7zPsJnkjEI5S3rRD5EheuCShZN3_AZMI-uAuo_w-rXZxw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic museum for interior or furniture designers - affordable entrance fee, easy to get around and good size.  Great selection of artifacts.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAFk7uibE2BctCM71UvAGnI&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-9-18"},{"Name":"Slex","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWbct4Aw-rcigEXdVAdlVF8WX_LBRo5joBinKWc1okbNC59YCsR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really adored this museum! I love baroque, rococo, and romantic era furniture so this place was a dream for me. They also had a temporary Versace fashion exhibit that was very fun to walk through. The best part? For whatever reason, the day we went it was free! (And it was a Saturday!)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICX65Ke8QE&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2024-10-20"},{"Name":"Itay Manor","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLm3lTIQwxXHsHsVtGHc45jPJyC9jww5b-ehfTAohoE_zvJ0w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A beautiful hidden museum! Located in a central location in the city! The entrance fee is ridiculous, the staff is so friendly and helpful! Inside the museum there is a great collection of arts & crafts from the early 20th century, combined with contemporary art! A recommended visit for design and aesthetics enthusiasts!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDI8brlmwE&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-4-10"},{"Name":"Liyi Z","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX03rtkx5M-0EbDuTkg4U7jHV7uqxUissG-GYmKapa9llLf964Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Tickets are cheap but they are free Thursdays 5-8 pm and Saturdays 2-3 pm. Be aware that exhibits are in Spanish or don't have much info. They also have some temporary exhibition space. I liked that the museum covered a good range of decorative arts from home goods to a whole replica kitchen or dining room. There's a Frank Lloyd Wright window (and chair), which is a shame to see not with light shining through it but a pleasant surprise anyway.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDI2aj1CA&fid=0x0:0x33df589b692a24dc"],"When":"2025-4-11"}]

[{"Name":"Eran Gordon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV9TCfglhZAbghI0RPu-m-ZPBi-FZdLJGAKjRajtReHhhnskaMd=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Nice place for kids, very gimicy but I think kids will like it, and The stuff at the tour was really nice.
They give a little gummy candy every room, and a small VR game at the end.
The price is 15€ per person.

I give 1 star cause they offered me a candy if I give them a good review.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQt1W2fArOblsDLJOasmnP&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-10-2"},{"Name":"Amanda Crook","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJI--3PEU6CVV2n1C01h-yKGKVCbVqSSw_3JMHuND5PXexbxg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Super cute and fun activity for kids. Takes about 30 minutes to complete. Take the slide down!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDTXKFaDAMvt_TxkkmUMJ5T&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-12-29"},{"Name":"Ana Reyes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWMwtoVpk1i3x_wiRJTSSM-Q4T6tR8QfucPFB3sfTx613SCfoKg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"1/5 stars*

Visited The Sweet Space museum in Madrid with my family, and unfortunately, it was a disappointing experience. We were expecting a fun and engaging time, but what we got was quite the opposite.

Firstly, the duration of the experience was misleading. They claimed it would be around 40-50 minutes long, but in reality, we were done in less than 15 minutes. It felt like a rushed and overpriced experience.

The attractions themselves were also quite boring. My kids and I didn't find them engaging or entertaining, and it seemed like they were just going through the motions.

The only highlights of our visit were the toboggan and the pool full of plastic balls. My kids seemed to enjoy those parts, but it wasn't enough to make up for the rest of the experience. My kids started crying ad they were looking for more things to do but by that time we were already by the exit.

Overall, I wouldn't recommend The Sweet Space museum to others. It felt like a scam, and the lack of transparency about the experience duration was frustrating. Maybe they can work on improving their attractions and being more honest about what visitors can expect.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCYATxwx6h_tORsyiHHhdMY&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-8-17"},{"Name":"Deborah Laks","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI3grBV_SHuXFTJao3Rutge7Qtph1-vhD5tPBYjbJL4SV7esFjt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A modern museum with a childhood twist. They give you sweets at every stop. When we went the entire once was decorated for Halloween. A nice place to enjoy with your kids.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPsDf66iV5GVDHHBkIymuV&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-10-28"},{"Name":"Maria Manduca","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLp8c_vw_Rx8IXWepmHa2TGsRjR-x5UtvczrqYHPDzQrUiybA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We spent a different day. My youngest son enjoyed it the most, especially because there were many other children there and he was able to find some company. We had a good time.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Péter Tircsi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVCRIZAxUz_FZObiv4KZppFU21hoNLseK-a-gh8Cb_X1Z9ISo_TyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Omg, it is such a great place to spend an hour or more. Each room is completely different, not only because of the art, but also the experience just to look at them, touch them, feel them, and have different activities, a lot to see and enjoy. They even decorated the whole place with Halloween decoration in October. We enjoyed the sweets as well which we got in the different rooms. The sour one was very sour! :) Highly recommended, don’t forget to wear clothes which have white writing/art on them because there are a lot of rooms where it is glowing in the dark :)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICXhYvSCg&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2024-10-18"},{"Name":"Susan Welsh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLI95JpjyBBVNTLcOjn5b433GELt5Zil0HtBBzWebLQRgjvGA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"A few random rooms with lights and painted walls and some decorations is meant to give feeling you are in space. You are given a total of 4 sweets 1 per room. Go down a slide with a small ball pit. Shop at the exit sells a few sweets and cheap toys. VR game is good but in a separate room after you’ve left. Nothing to do with sweets or space or a museum.
Really disappointing don’t waste your money!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Alksjndrs F","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKoOWDYxbjIE-rwV5dmL-TzkAaglNsNFj1MjdX8QuGJ54Nfi_E=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Quite a rip-off. Paid 40€ for 2 adults and a three year old. It’s not really a playground more like a wannabe instagram picture place. In everything the kids get sweets. Total tour takes you maybe 20 minutes. We had a group of 20 including kids which was way too many people for some of the rooms.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCeCydmfjMefZ3TaCcEAfVN&fid=0x0:0x3c057d0909d857d3"],"When":"2025-8-6"}]

La gestión cultural se hace visible por todos lados. Hay en el museo la exposición de sala permanente sobre el origen del municipio y sus tres mil años de historia,lo cuál es increíble.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Criben Ridro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWbsI4aZVYfPeQ-UjQS3ChrB0AnUUXBy6RZgkJ5KEGbw5qUDe6q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo en el que puedes conocer la historia de la ciudad de Torrejon, en nuestro caso pudimos acudir a una visita teatralizada para conocer su historia a través de emblemáticos personajes famosos en Torrejon de Ardoz. Sin duda una agradable experiencia que nos gustó mucho.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDhtaTIOw&fid=0x0:0xa8481830423fbcd1"],"When":"2023-3-14"},{"Name":"Edward Puelles Valle","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUu_kIT47KJY7y6zDVya4LoTehSBXUNlw2hkViLWYggc2X4L64C-g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un Museo 🖼️ que te brinda acceso libre para poder apreciar parte de la historia de Torrejón de Ardóz, desde muchos años atrás desde sus ancestros del Siglo XVIII en promedio
Además con murales y también restos arqueológicos
Cuenta con pantallas para poder ilustrar al visitante de lo que fue la emancipación y la vida en su evolución en sus diferentes etapas
Con un resúmen de su población desde 1526 con 600 pobladores y 140 viviendas, hasta la última encuesta de población del 2021 de 132,771 personas y una población en viviendas de 51,547 viviendas
Muy bonito y muy ordenado con buena luz y con secuencias muy bien indicadas
Recomendado visitarlo en los días de atención","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIClo8moBw&fid=0x0:0xa8481830423fbcd1"],"When":"2023-11-25"},{"Name":"Jörge","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXeWEXqdr5t3OlbRokb_Dhsg38mDGwuZo9JWUkx-spF0MuHs0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo inaugurado en el año 2011, dedicado a explicar la historia del municipio de Torrejón de Ardoz. Se encuentra situado en el antiguo edificio de la Herrería, y cuenta con tres plantas: en la primera se encuentra una exposición sobre la arqueología de la localidad, principalmente con los descubrimientos realizados en el yacimiento de Soto de Henares, contando con reproducciones de los hallazgos y piezas originales. En la segunda planta se encuentra la exposición que abarca desde la Edad Media hasta la actualidad, mientras que en la tercera se encuentra un espacio destinado a las exposiciones temporales","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDN1aDM2AE&fid=0x0:0xa8481830423fbcd1"],"When":"2024-1-24"},{"Name":"Luis Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW235KrchEY7jAU2usKj6vTT7QhXWWMu0VoebVS3GjLutwJAk3y=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Lugar de arte y cultura. Exposiciones temporales interesantes. En el sótano tienen una exposición permanente de Gigantes y Cabezudos, que se puede visitar los domingos.","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"James Brazier","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXExyHDqgeVtyTfRwdYDNjhh92rDitOTYOTqH7gNmxYJi_DMmB9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The collection is very diverse: paintings, sculptures, jewelry, weapons, decorative arts, and rare books. You can see works by Goya, El Greco, and many European artists.

The Leonardo da Vinci painting is worth a look. The Adolescent Saviour is claimed to be an original by the great man however perhaps it was a pupil from his workshop. Either way it’s has to be considered a Leonardesque.

Overall, it’s a peaceful, high-quality museum with lots of interesting objects.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhALZc-cMUzDXPXX-dK08vO6&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-11-30"},{"Name":"eh m","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJDc4S4npu6fVJbaYEi1ib1lumKNzCIEZg_ypimQordOgj_nw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I have visited the Museo Lázaro Galdiano twice, and it continues to impress me each time. The museum offers an excellent price-to-value experience, given the extraordinary range of works on display. The beautifully designed rooms and the architecture of the building alone are worth a visit.

It is truly remarkable what José Lázaro Galdiano achieved in his lifetime and the exceptional collection he assembled. If you are in Madrid, it is without doubt a must-see.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD59cElIrKw5ykWjnB0Raw-&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-9-24"},{"Name":"Linda Bailey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV13-ZLd5E8gbX7oVINRLkobXoW7aOrzbAdXFra4V8v2e0Q96I=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Incredible museum with the collection of José Lázaro Galdiano. There are lockers before ticket booth but they seemed not so secure to us.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCqp7OhhcM2qVuu-pdR4iWX&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2025-9-27"},{"Name":"Song","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhvoqfI6DYGerqYUa0bfmANUUJ7mMuHOF55sTOJDSNa_D1MQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic collection in a stunningly beautiful building. I am glad I didn’t miss this hidden treasure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBT85E3GPBi34tpXYdwOZar&fid=0x0:0xaeee248571d949a9"],"When":"2026-2-4"},{"Name":"Anton N.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI4TJjJDuMPiDu-_95c8DFbSUKPJ_ercGHl_xr2ZKQp1F_CYA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I absolutely loved visiting the Lazaro Galdiano Museum! descriptions: Museum relating city's history back to the Paleolithic period via artifacts, maps & paintings.
owner: {"id":"106333832438272038625","name":"Museo de San Isidro. Los Orígenes de Madrid (Owner)","link":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/106333832438272038625"}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ A Hidden Gem of Madrid History – And It’s Free!

If you’re looking for a high-quality cultural experience in the heart of the city, Museo de San Isidro is a fantastic find. It’s a wonderful way to spend an hour or two, especially if you need a break from the Madrid sun in a cool, air-conditioned space.

What to Expect:
The museum is very well-organized and showcases the history of Madrid from prehistoric times to the semi-modern era. It’s filled with fascinating artifacts, including:

Impressive Fossils: Large-scale displays that bring ancient history to life.

Archaeological Finds: Beautifully preserved pottery, tools, and sculptures.

Interactive Exhibits: Plenty of high-quality, interactive elements that make the history engaging for all ages.

Why It’s a Great Visit:
Bilingual Displays: One of the best parts is that all the signage and information are in both Spanish and English, making it very accessible for international visitors.

Quiet & Peaceful: Unlike the major galleries in Madrid, this museum is not crowded, allowing you to slow down and really absorb the details.

Welcoming Atmosphere: The staff is extremely helpful and welcoming from the moment you walk in.

Bonus – Free Entry: Admission is completely FREE, which is incredible given the quality of the exhibits.

Final Thoughts:
Located in the charming Plaza de San Andrés, this museum is a "must" for history buffs or anyone wanting to understand the origins of Madrid. Whether you are admiring the beautiful courtyard or the ancient mammoth fossils, you won't be disappointed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD4JZ891c15EjgOGHvfwluC&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-1-25"},{"Name":"PG G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-KZn3KzHQeyJSRbIkb3rLdzUVXN_dKo03beVE4Nscw7tU7g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It's free!
Friendly staff.
Lovely courtyard garden.
Fascinating history of Madrid and lots and lots of relics, fossils, ancient tools and artefacts.
We stumbled across this museum after a disappointing attempt to see the nearby Cathedral (closed indefinitely for renovations). We ended up in there for nearly 2 hours!
Highlights:
Mammoth tusk fossil
Stone age tools and flints
Bronze urns
Roman mosaic floor
3-d model of old Madrid walled city
Statues
And it's free!
Please buy a fridge magnet or souvenir to show support!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD_89Y-hArWf1UTpnU6V3iP&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"Kevin Yeung","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfr8VfoLnrWAatiNzspZuHOsAYPQ0rQ8QcqYSnL_8Cqky7zGB38w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very informative.   The displayed items are well Organized and described.   The staff and security guards are very friendly as well.  The 2nd floor is used for temporary exhibition which is also worth visiting.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCFJh5jJ6aWd5OfSdJnkwty&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2025-11-4"},{"Name":"Nil Alpaslan Kasimoglu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKlCG1yNzuz1OIRPW8keszyu1bWND86O_CgjpWHOtrPExVwhA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a museum with free entrance that displays the story of Madrid starting from the stone age till today. Easy to see around, good descriptions and some interactive spots.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDXUwoOz43pGxcId1mE60o8&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-1-24"},{"Name":"Julio N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMN4zqvkcTuhncYG16PdKq-oimRWaSzkXPSI5EJIoQBj46DmE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It is a very interesting museum about the history of Madrid since pre-historic times.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhACO6SQdp6WxTPzbpO-kpvh&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2026-2-22"},{"Name":"Jane C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWD2fict4yUr0RLT0psUvEs8irrJsa5SXmgjXPupEeil-Og1xK7wQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We were in Madrid for an overnight visit and while waiting on our friend to arrive, we decided to explore La Latina barrio. Having stopped for a drink on the terrace opposite the museum, we then stopped in as we’re interested in prehistoric history. The museum is great, the people welcoming and the exhibits are very well done. Add in that the signs are in both Spanish and English and the admission is FREE, and you’ve got yourself a wonderful way to spend an hour out of the hot sun in the air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAl9ZcABAnCAaTrnm8Yc553&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2025-6-27"},{"Name":"Tracey Johnson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXwskhnIlnEeo-n_0YSv-LxRrghFV-m3Br-gt01qKASRTdlV9o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A charming and insightful museum that delves into the fascinating origins of Madrid! Museo de San Isidro offers a thoughtful journey through the city’s early history - from prehistoric times to the foundation of the modern capital. The exhibits are well-organized, and the building itself, with its lovely courtyard and peaceful atmosphere, adds to the experience.
It’s not a huge museum, but it’s full of interesting artifacts and stories that bring Madrid’s beginnings to life. Entry is free, which makes it even more worthwhile. Some displays could benefit from more English translations, but overall it’s a great cultural stop for anyone interested in history. A quiet gem tucked away in the heart of old Madrid - definitely worth visiting!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"J C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXj0TgkTNAuGwMesWnHLHh3gZP_MQOoaZdzbuIwp4tG_MvQdIP7w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A local walking tour guide recommended this quaint, charming museum for a visit. Entry is free. You have nothing to lose and a whole lot of great information to gain in the archeology and living history of Madrid city - less royalty and politics, and more on how locals lived through the centuries.

After or before this visit, go to the local mercado (public market) nearby for a stroll and a bite & drink. Experience where locals eat and shop daily.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICHtdvDRA&fid=0x0:0x404feb59a6e23419"],"When":"2024-9-7"}]

[{"Name":"Daniel Alqsous","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKw4rPao_9b4IaexHZnv92NM7flFJ-AguwiAkwBhOkX2SRiRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the coolest museums i have ever visited. Honestly really cool. It has 2 floors, with the ground floor being more or less basic, but once you go to the first, there are 6 rooms, each with its own story and description, and they are all so unique - it is all quite incredible. Then in the 2nd floor, there is a journey in and of itself. So many rooms, so many cool things to see, it is nothing short of spectacular.

Also, students have free entry!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB-Xb1BgfgezadMSHinMeIK&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-19"},{"Name":"Roberto Alonso","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJsK6knQRzXtOKSNodgOVqK9_qVAJQVVLWksEZp11iJw85uRg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such an amazing place. Very well preserved, like machine travel, I can really imagine how the people lived there back in time. Super cheap, really worth the visit. The museum workers are very very friendly and kind, they even make a game to keep my kid busy! We went around 12 on a weekday and we entered right away, no line up!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCmz5DxtyISIVZ0iBvqrDAq&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-14"},{"Name":"Denisa Boar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUz4j1MccDX5a9w1bCEZ9L9NF0O4fR0gp_RcR6pqDA-cwFMYfY2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic place, filled with history and culture. Gratiously preserved. Compared to other such establishments from Vienna and Budapest, this offered much more. Only 6 persons at a time, plenty of space to enjoy and breathable even during busy Christmas period. 2.5 euro for the audio guide which was perfect.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCS1jdiN3EOrCUpIVqSZy4D&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-1"},{"Name":"Cansu M.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJSODTQLbwWbxxIrjR2ZzHKegLDqIqmrjqlh8TQRt2kRdKByg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A beautiful hidden gem in Madrid. Elegant, peaceful, and like stepping into a 19th-century palace. Absolutely worth a visit. We visited at 17:00 on Thursday and the entrance that time was free. We had to wait around 10-15 minutes though to enter.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC9F5wHmHVCRrknC2XVkZuO&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Pei C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjULBN9fst04m1x5gWQB5UkBv0a2ijNs0E6qe-10r07nWai24832=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It’s a hidden gem in Madrid because it’s small and not as famous as other museums or palace! But it’s stunning and beautiful! Absolutely luxurious!

Another good thing is they only allow 40 visitors on time so there’s no crowds. The cons is you have to queue outside a bit longer

They have free entry slot during 6-7pm on Thursdays but since the ticket is only 3€, not worth to queue and squeeze the visit time ( because once you enter, they are about to close)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAjJq0vn4YjUXExaFJ60FQg&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-10-19"},{"Name":"Galina Strekalova","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJLaWdtg_g2DT5-dkryTOlAcyFpNSXySBRsYiKEROF5cPVkXQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely breathtaking place.
All the enthusiastic words in the world wouldn’t be enough to describe this beauty.
We waited in line for about 40 minutes, but it was absolutely worth it!
Come early so you can enjoy all the masterpieces for longer. And the price is just as delightful — only €3!
A place that deserves a 100% recommendation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCtbkgeFRjqyR6JOKB6lP9j&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-10-16"},{"Name":"Yulia Alexandrovna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWY5tSODWWVGrpgiTA-avxaEm0Z_IURkK764c6l9it8LWzalL3_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Absolutely beautiful museum and its collection!
Some practicalities can help enjoying the museum even more - audio guide via headsets so that your hands are free. As otherwise you hold everything in your hands (not even a small bag is allowed inside) which is very inconvenient","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAxn3jfe8g1l_7U_yWQmKWA&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"eh m","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJDc4S4npu6fVJbaYEi1ib1lumKNzCIEZg_ypimQordOgj_nw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo de Cerralbo is one of those places where you can genuinely imagine how people once lived. The rooms are preserved with such care that it feels natural to step into the daily life of another era. It’s fascinating to walk through the house, from the grand staircase to the quieter corners, and the clear descriptions make it easy to follow the story of the family and the collection. A charming and atmospheric place;  highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBThl4J40qEvXdEKoJhUZ1d&fid=0x0:0x5d762ebfbf6373a8"],"When":"2025-11-29"}]

[{"Name":"Nicole Cunningham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX9x3i6hs_OpoaEbv5FevvBdCk2ErTm9nzClEO16x0ZRSwsyqR30Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It was a nice museum and we had a sweet tour guide. Only 4/5 because it is what it is without a lot of flare. If you have free time the I formation was interesting and it’s worth a visit especially since it’s free.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDksaG0yr_PirLv8mdnteui&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2025-11-7"},{"Name":"Pong Lenis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWmFxgCYM6gEAt1jIKoZP8ptzgNZL-9zzzKJy5MkI8Asohd2Jid=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What an amazing time, lots to see, very beautiful architecture and buildings. The exhibitions were absolutely stunning, they are very impressive and informative. This is a great place for groups of all ages, lots to see and do. Very clean, staff is very friendly and knowledgeable, lots of places to eat and very nice gift shop. The price was very reasonable, would most definitely recommend this place to family and friends. We had a wonderful time and were glad we stopped by!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC5nLutSg&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2023-10-11"},{"Name":"Dalila Mesta","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHTDyQeyc2dn57Z7UrayDN2Y0oZVxuMSL4Qcth4eWoQSu3Zrjd=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great museum! And free
All the staff was great and very friendly.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDFZMsVrcyOEFBkKeNygclt&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2026-1-30"},{"Name":"lm c","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWISjXvaWyNRz6ehSH7UZuvO9goelI8qsATNy1SgZ6nwZETrl7w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We stumble upon this museum not knowing what to expect. There is no admission fees and no lineups. It is so much bigger than we expect. There is so much to see and the collections is quite vast. I thought it would be similar to the Mint museum in Atlanta, but it has so much more. Stamps, Medals, Coins, and the histories of how money is form in Spain. This is worthwhile if you have time in Madrid. Most displays have English. The gift shop provides first day cover for coins and some stamps.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDdpf2r6AE&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2024-2-25"},{"Name":"Fahad Butt","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUwUTQwiBUM0rOwhvhcAjPsICNs5orUnpOKJWsr8lqTujUh0gBT=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I write a review using google maps website which is not showing here.

To this museum, I will give 6 stars if there is option :)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBUmzSoXSdCSTW-nsyBY3S8&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2025-10-4"},{"Name":"Michele Rookwood (Michele R)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUwCkdF1cwed2arZ2XRNUDx7jQsZQin8RmV8QMi5dMmki_umrDA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"You can visit here free you just show your ID.  It’s very dim inside  to preserve the exhibits so hard for taking photos  .  Signs are in Spanish but the newer exhibits have Spanish and English translations.  There are audio guides with a QR code . In the rooms are collections of banknotes and other means of payment, lottery and games,  Olympic medals and tools for the manufacture used by the Fábrica Nacional de Moneda y Timbre-Real Casa de la Moneda so much to see.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID70PGboQE&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2024-8-20"},{"Name":"Cleofe Albano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW-6Hgk8oEowUDYmKWOkTfBCLiCjhDqvDkOXmwIxrlzce757dcj=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing museum. Clean, spacious, and full of amazing coins from the past. Everything is in spanish, but hey, get Google translator! My one wish is  that  the videos could have more languages or subtitles and the museum shop had other items like stamps and postage stamps. Will highly recommend","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDevszUiwE&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2022-10-11"},{"Name":"iRINA Ya","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKfdQdPt5QcRciwrRie-N4Wl-ZotydtoKTy6r7tgOWAKq6kvQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Interesting storytelling from why the money was needed, Greece, Rome to Bourbons... Curious. For those who are interested in this subject. Maybe not that interesting for children. The temporary exhibitions were also dedicated to linogravure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD51LOwgm5M0eGrB0rBN7fn&fid=0x0:0xb6d1211bf0134cad"],"When":"2025-10-17"}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"Lorenzo Berardinelli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLQYZfs7aGmPhWV6GArevvBQrV3lL4dmCAQU_1872nRZD-NgA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I visited the Museum of Illusions in Madrid with my girlfriend, and it was a fun experience overall. However, I think it’s more enjoyable if you go in a group of more than two people, since many of the photo spots are designed for pairs or groups. Inside, there aren’t that many interactive activities, but the main issue is definitely the number of children running around — though of course, you can’t really ask them not to come! Other than that, we had a good time. I’d recommend going with friends or people you know you can have fun with anywhere.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDmml8-lSdI7MJDcz6dCc1F\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-10-27"},{"Name":"Darren Beck","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLRfTQ5lzSivZGEcb4EWCur2uNoffJUe10rBNwzOdNobY-mUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A little small but worth an hour or so if you want a break from the heat. Note that it definitely skews more toward a younger crowd, and there were a lot of families with little kids there. As a group of two adults though, it was still a fun experience. I’m sure the kids had a great time as well. Staff were great also. Give it a try if you need a break from the sightseeing and just want some fun relaxation and air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCDZBKUvmAhb_LOAn8QK7LQ\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-13"},{"Name":"rated by maryam • food critic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV2rFU889_uyIosz_7bpffSFpmpz0-gKcaVKqZvHb7h1qD_qhXV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely entertaining from start to finish! The Museum of Illusions in Madrid kept us all fully engaged — we could’ve easily spent two hours exploring. The installations are clever, interactive, and truly high quality. My kids had a blast, and honestly, so did I. Every room is picture perfect, and the staff made sure the flow was easy and fun. Whether you’re going with kids or just want to play with perspective and be wowed, this spot delivers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCvIn2cbOwD1xlT2Bju8D5l\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-1"},{"Name":"Alex S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuqMxZHWW4Q3oWg1MozJ5EDSP48AY-vf9ImsmDzcUsLKEKjbGa=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wow! What a great museum! If you want to find where the magic is at in Madrid, look no further! Offering many illusions for you to try out and capture, this museum is incredibly fun for everyone of all ages except people who otherwise have vertigo, as some of the illusions you will have to walk through may not be good for people with vertigo.\nIf you are wanting a fun activity to do with your kids, it’s a great place! Depending on your pace, you could be at this museum for a while. My favourite illusion was the vortex, as it is a really cool experience and it really makes you feel like you are spinning in an actual vortex. If that’s your thing, it is the last illusion downstairs at the very end of the room. This museum is also not far from the train line, so you can travel here easily.\nIn sum, if you’re wanting something to do while you’re in Madrid, come here!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAiCzHJZBmXN2Y3VDQxPYol\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-7"},{"Name":"Michael Stockdale","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIrWLzicQpgC3hAFUv5MqBQNE3eX1_sKxt7Pa3MR8iF60jyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"It was okay but l was hoping to see more than l did for the entry price.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoNl4gi2TxojBRMWk7xy-N\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-12-15"},{"Name":"Rebecca Sirois","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVZzU3H_Gmj---yW_cc8WSqFd5G5oCZ4-hT-20m9bPEiVF3i3R=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a fun experience with the kids. Definitely buy tickets in advance, they don't let you in early so show up right on time. Staff were helpful for picture taking otherwise a fun place to wander through at your leisure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCS7-Y2SbFgXGvCL3e_1CBg\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"Joel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWl5821tyzJrwoJ9WtZ4ckHFtm77vetb8svyUaCTvs_yjbDKrzH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had such a great time at the Museum of Illusions in Madrid! I went with my two kids and they absolutely loved it — from the mind-bending exhibits to all the fun photo ops, there was never a dull moment. A big shoutout to Roberto, who went above and beyond. He was incredibly kind and patient, explaining how the illusions worked in a way my kids could understand, and even helped us take some amazing photos. He showed us the best angles and made sure we got the most out of each exhibit. Definitely recommend for a fun, family-friendly outing. So much fun and lots of laughs!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA41UAbda0q4GHtxLgczpgq\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"David Sargent","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNd7Vx1pxVZ2hd91K_Xk_NBWo4ZUDgz8hEItPBONm_A7r1mRme=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love finding unusual museums, and I read about this one on WikiTravel when I had some spare time on a recwnt business trip. This museum was an absolute delight! My only regret was that I was alone, as a lot of the displays are interactive and the couples and groups were having so much fun watching and photographing each other and srading places and such that I decided that someday I need to go back there with at least one other person who hasn't seen it before. Fingers crossed for another trip to Madrid one of these days!!","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"assaf attas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVg3Ciyy5UkW5T80wqEE_QTphB3Y67Wy8J3oZQ_eFdUiav10w0Oyg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The staff is very nice, but the place itself not very interesting. Most of the stations involves reading,  few interactive attractions which were mediocre. It is nice for smaller ages I guess, we didn't find the place to be very funny, not really worth the price. Maybe an option for a rainy day and you are in the area. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Dong Cai","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVDzBsXATZ4chn3Go4FUKsm03lk7jcwsyJ89uTw1eNKA2ebF_c=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"The experience in itself is good.  However, given the small size of the venue and things to do, definitely not worth the 68€ for a family of 5.  Everything could be done and seen in literally 15-20 mins.  Definitely felt like a tourist trap for this venue.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Stefan Nilles","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVcSebPbMGAP-j9hMDMpSkJM2OSZiScSw7Jd2G1MSWm5hmuLuIdjw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Do not visit this place unless your are a Spanish nationalist family with very young children!

The place is catering exclusively to children to teach them about the embroidery on the torrreros' uniform, the tradition of spanish tiles as an art from and other mundane details of Spanish culture that are presented as something Spanish kids should be proud of.

The glacially paced narrative of the projected information is in Spanish (for 3 year olds) and you can read the English translation projected to the wall.

Total waste of money and time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBxSdMF_EItVZ0NhATVpwun&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Chris Tomkins","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUMdh9K_6GffzOUidLsxnoeKD-7jw1e7YUvNARYkIb04iAytgI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really good set up. Very engaging immersive experience for the artists. The bit at the end was just novel fun.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCHot09waWMZrBDGpS-w-Z3&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-28"},{"Name":"Jonas Montvidas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXykj1DBG41wM8onrJXAQ3usyMsllep8989BjgjWIqriCxo8UQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"It is a hoax. You pay 18 euros to get into two rooms - one bigger and one smaller. The same story about The Last Supper by Davinci goes on a loop in the bigger room, and there are two/three activities for the children in the smaller room. Two short hallways with lights are the main attraction of this place. But even these hallways are just a cheap copy of other museums worldwide that offer better experiences. Not worth 18 euros","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCoqoaZkAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-4-16"},{"Name":"Shalom N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXjmGFJHDuC19RcGGg0lG7mHAePo2CjbG7fd4Pk9sPEsG8DxAUj=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It was cute for the 10 minutes it lasted for, you can tell that this place was made for influencers to come and take a quick video so others will feel the need to come.

It was one main exhibition room where they just projected some stuff on the wall, then another room where you get to view terrible VR and do some coloring, i got pretty pictures i guess but would i recommend? No because its not giving you value for your money

It is giving very much tourist trap because you leave feeling unsatisfied because it can literally take you 5 minutes to go through it all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQ58b0UQ&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-3-7"},{"Name":"Jose Maria Tijerina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUN2vlCdnJoVVzCMvczORJZNy6fp097DcqnE5mvO5JL7UyEJ4mK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The immersive experience is very interesting. When I visited, the experience was about the work of Klimt and Van Gogh. I went on a Monday so it was not full. The extra activities besides the immersive experience are more for kids than adults. There are a  virtual painting wall, last for 3 minutes, drawings for kids and virtual reality headsets that were not working when i attended. The narration on the immersive experience is ominous Spanish language, so if you cannot follow it then you would not get  part of the experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCQHraYDrmqxt2VPIpl0y2E&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2025-7-13"},{"Name":"Ariannaly Pea Martínez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUHlslcBXwCCEYwj-22n12iqaNzJJSfd6xSJOjFHvdkhRKV2mc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It’s a pretty nice and neat experience. Visuals and music were the best of it, although the interactive room was a bit mid imo. Either way I’d sit hours and hours just to enjoy the Spirit of Japan showcase","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC3vuqCmAE&fid=0x0:0xcab6a6b2c4f8d444"],"When":"2024-11-6"},{"Name":"Doron Privé","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKkdtQAXsQ4Kx4kWjv6_tOt_smbLwFWiIFkEVxSgS1lbwIt1iE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went there with two friends and it was very disappointing. The tour last maximum 10min and was very expansive. If you are not a Spanish speaker, it’s just a waste of money because they are projecting a kind of movie during the second part of the tour and the it’s 100% in Spanish.
The museum is a first corridor with lights and mirror on 10 meters (it’s the  only cool thing you will see), after that there’s the Spanish movie projected on walls, ground and ceiling and then it’s finished. I told you, it last 10 minutes and cost ~20€/person.","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Helen Stasa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkfbRD5S6JkCLjo4woGkC6XUh2i9JY7Y96xNg9wsDsS9wFsiE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"It’s an interesting idea, but there’s definitely some improvements that could be made.
1) In booking the tour, you’re obliged to have a back-and-forward WhatsApp exchange with an employee about the time. Surely this could be automated? Similarly, the tone of the WhatsApp messages is very patronising- they treat you like you lack basic comprehension skills e.g. “Be advised that the museum is in MADRID.” Yes. We know.
2) The staff member was clearly bored and uninterested and going through the motions. The tour started 10 minutes late (the bossy WhatsApp messages insisted we MUST be early), and, upon noticing that I had an accent, the staff member told me “The tour is in Spanish. It’s not in English.” I told her (in Spanish) that understood that, and that I can understand Spanish. I considered the conversation closed, but at the end of the tour, she again insisted on addressing me in English in front of everyone. I found this impolite, and bad customer service. In addition, she had a bored expression on her face the whole time, which was hardly fun for the kids.
3) The rooms are full of interesting things, but you don’t get given enough time to look at them. Instead, you’re rushed from room to room.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICn09fydA&fid=0x0:0x7180acca61fb61bb"],"When":"2024-9-29"},{"Name":"Carol Matiz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ1TvbhuEQcxzksgRIkjdPZG2rSXSfB66q3q_pi3jXKZDwTMJY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Our visit was great, we love it! The storyteller was excellent just a little fast telling the story, but we still had a very good experience our little one enjoyed it. And they don't let you take pictures or videos.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Amnet Pantaleon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVdOrQg7I6VMdSmP1_34UfqqjOYsmOKaVqCJOzPiGHpUBuQY9oI=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful place and loving staff.very magical for the little ones and the entire family. Highly recommended.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sarah B","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWJbDlKPDDo4d5sMWGfEHsjEEpymmi8Jw8jqCw_HBuUDm8pw8T_qg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We visited with our three children (ages 10, 9 and 7) after a visit to the Royal Palace.

Initially told the 5pm tour full so we were going to book for 6pm but it became clear we were English so we were allowed to go on our own as likely we don't speak enough Spanish to benefit from guided tour.

The museum is located in a sort of mini shopping arcade on the main road between Sol and Opera. It's easy to miss. We went up to the museum and one of the staff members spoke good English so showed us into the cartoon presentation (in Spanish but understood enough that we got the story) she also gave us a book with the English translation in to read too. We are then given a map (in English) to spot the key items as you move to the three rooms.

It's a very quirky museum full of legend about the tooth mouse and the children enjoyed clambering through the little tunnels and seeing if they started getting an itchy nose. We liked the biscuit tin room and the special post box and the tunnel where they found the special coins left for them.

I would say that at 5 euro per person it didn't feel like amazing value for a family of five BUT that said the children loved it and the member of staff engaged with them beautifully and helped us to find the mouse door out on the main road.

Great little museum if you have children similar ages to ours but not the best value thing you will do during a day in Madrid.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"BasicReviews","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUm35sAVzX2zZyFrASlJrXPsSi8kGFv4Qx_OGrBZHL2J2GI-HxlQA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome place. Nice staff. Good story.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEI7xkqi1_Zz5Bg&fid=0x0:0x7180acca61fb61bb"],"When":"2025-5-23"},{"Name":"Lole Ramirez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLvEBMqQIRYo0vU_HXUyb6ZBsN_Bl8-JeCVOFMX-lOykIJc7g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It was ok, but not very interactive and our 5 year old did not engage, which is odd as she is every curious.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Monica Lidia Rodriguez Estrada","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxxKYl6aehcsVQdmZchgXc-dHiaqbW_yDPd0kkbQNCOqZAaDUK=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The staff was nice although the museum was quite small not something I was expecting. I went with my 17 month old and 6 year old.  I was advised by the guide to please step out of the explanation in  case  the baby would start crying, it made sense since it was crowded. and hardly had any room to walk. I would not recommend it for babies unless an adult can stay with them outside.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Cian Donohoe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUpR7dgPhfhDsUehrwXOBt8f4Z9NWaR-YlOEIb9iyx_rYXfHSAF=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic food and atmosphere. The waiter Juan was incredible, very nice, very funny, and made sure we were enjoying ourselves. Would definitely recommend for an evening meal and some drinks","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Kelly Sidney","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVuUqfKbkpL015_LwC-awHUYq0K5---lp_AbgGyRQ5gkxchN7hR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a beautiful collection of Picasso, Salvator Dali, and Joan Miró pieces… amongst several other talented artists ! I preferred this museum significantly over the Prado; it had much better organization and ease to find pieces. The Guernica in person was magnificent. It is a very large museum which you can spend at least 2-3 hours inside. If you are on a time crunch, the temporary contemporary exhibitions were pleasant as well but less a standout compared to the permanent collection.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhALWZzEXq-0QAfRjRAOzQ64&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-2-6"},{"Name":"Alex Veziro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV_prC4j81QgIKfVHsb1zgnDfGKJ6o0QVeve6gytMISddtq28WetA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Museo Reina Sofía is one of those museums that leaves a strong impression even if you’re not particularly into modern art. The space itself is beautiful, with plenty of natural light and a calm flow that makes the visit feel relaxed rather than tiring, even if you stay for a while. The mix between the historic building and the more contemporary elements works really well and fits the character of the exhibitions.
The main highlight is of course Picasso’s Guernica, which feels completely different in person and has a kind of intensity that makes you stop and take your time with it. Beyond that, the collection as a whole is quite engaging, with some works that make you think and others that you simply enjoy visually, without feeling the need to overanalyze them.
Overall, it’s definitely a museum worth visiting if you’re in Madrid. It doesn’t feel boring or cold, and you leave with the sense that you’ve experienced something meaningful, even if you don’t remember every artist or movement afterward. It’s also great for a more laid-back visit, without the pressure of having to see absolutely everything.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDqj6phukx3ZgfKwfFS17Oq&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Tata Kutaladze","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK0DVJn4bsWYsDSJlAwlQE2Cku3Sdvyo6gUgL7mt6PQUegQAg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My main reason for visiting the Reina Sofía Museum was Picasso and his Guernica, and it was absolutely worth it. Seeing Guernica in person is a powerful and emotional experience — the scale, details, and meaning are far more intense than in any book or photo.
Dali and Miro are  impressive as well.
A practical tip: buy your ticket online. The entrance line is much shorter compared to waiting in front of the ticket office, which saves a lot of time. Overall, a must-visit museum in Madrid, especially for anyone interested in modern art and Picasso’s legacy.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDA4ckpcuwpD0Hvb1bUJ9Ky&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-1-14"},{"Name":"I Berezner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVpuR88LJbO2mGM0lgRoSSgPom1qNaDTbZc_75JhdnFpfi3w3af7A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía stands out as Madrid's premier modern art destination, housing an exceptional collection that spans surrealism to abstract expressionism. Picasso's monumental Guernica commands the spotlight, its raw power amplified by surrounding works that contextualize the Spanish Civil War's devastation. Dalí's melting clocks, Miró's playful abstractions, and international gems like Rothko create a dynamic narrative of 20th-century creativity and turmoil. The renovated hospital building blends airy glass extensions with historic stone, offering light-filled galleries and serene courtyards perfect for reflection. Navigation is intuitive, though the sheer scale rewards a focused itinerary—prioritize floors 2 and 4 for highlights. Temporary exhibits often steal the show, adding fresh energy. Despite occasional crowds around Guernica, the museum's thoughtful spacing ensures an immersive experience. Reina Sofía isn't just art—it's a profound dialogue with history, making it indispensable for cultured travelers","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDB_o3oMjw8-sZEWxSHwF_s&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"Asher Mendelsohn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKqRvYW-a_lSUaf5iTtmw6DBfVcf7TfVR491K28IHEfLs9SorY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wonderful museum. Very easy to get in even though it was a crowded Sunday morning. You can pre-buy your art pass (covers 2 other museums as well) or buy a pass at the gate. The queue to buy the pass was longer to enter the museum, so I recommend pre buying the art pass online. The museum is quite big, and there are multiple floors, however when I visited only floors 1 and 2 were open. The world famous Guernica painting by Pablo Picasso is located on the second floor. I like how there’s a cloakroom where you can store all your valuables, and bags. There’s also lots of toilets around the museum. The museum contains a large amount of paintings and other work too, many of them being from Picasso, Dali, and more. But the most popular of all is Guernica, and that can be seen by the sheer amount of people standing around it. Overall museum is great and I enjoy the large collection of art.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAXUzvqZ48DFCyk6ib2H715&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-22"},{"Name":"Nicos Stathis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKc-rWmYLiJLBz4mtw01fjprIyd-zvV9OdX_F75kJp_dWx4RvY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I believe one of the most important museums in the world.

It is not just the Guernica, which is astonishing and pivotal indeed, the whole exhibition is a great story telling of how things evolved from late 1800s to mid 1900s, and many artworks and references of some of the most iconic modernism enthusiasts, for example Le Corbusier.

I visited on a Monday, I cannot say it was crowded.

The facade restoration keeps going, not affecting the visitor’s experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBd0vRqhvICgS52QH0HDzpT&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"Simona","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXIxl36BDvWwaZaehYUjB_o5qPqD55SL2EAL5iQDj96hxaCIkEj=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An amazing gallery, which concentrates on spanish art, but with some very interesting and valuable international links too.
I went on a sunday in the free hour 12:30-14:15 for which i would recommend being there 30 minute earlier since obviously many people do this🤭. Still despite the long queue, people were getting in pretty quickly.
When in, the permanent paintings, distributed in the halls by style (cubism, post cubism, surrealism, etc.) were on the second floor (3rd and 4th floor were temporarily closed).
On the first floor there was an amazing exhibit by Maruja Mallo (i’ll post photos of some of her paintings).","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCbj5NOy8CEzlG6-hG8v7uT&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-11-3"},{"Name":"Neda Pavlovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWa59zCZJWVxSL2SdtoKVSif7DYFnayRKCeB7JBshDyK-fQVdrC=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Reina Sofía is a museum you shouldn’t skip in Madrid. While many go straight to the Prado, Reina Sofía offers a modern, refreshing art experience with masterpieces like Picasso’s Guernica. A big plus is that photography is allowed, unlike in the Prado, which makes the visit more relaxed and enjoyable. The museum café also serves great coffee, perfect for a quick break. Overall, a must-visit spot for art lovers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPVrVcWtyDWZpO40p6k-l5&fid=0x0:0x309814d159babf71"],"When":"2025-12-8"}]

Hay tiendas para comprar recuerdos.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhADctlqaB7I5trl6htHd0gY\u0026fid=0x0:0xb02e9a979e8218c4"],"When":"2025-12-11"},{"Name":"Elizabeth Paula","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLklQMKKPgeaXkp7TeH-c_ZN3y1bHR7Ixpspp-hlG1lRLSINg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El guía fue encantador y te explica todo muy bien , ha estado en varios países de africanos durante varios años. El museo es precioso y acogedor.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICXsonGIQ\u0026fid=0x0:0xb02e9a979e8218c4"],"When":"2024-10-14"},{"Name":"Patricia P.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJPZBx5TAO5H2A3N7BF1OaeLz8YG81eP6_-G0dfNAHtDpo1Tg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fue una visita maravillosa por eso pongo las 5 estrellas. Fui con un grupo de personas entre 70 y 90 años. Salieron encantados y compraron un detalle en la tienda para colaborar con nuevos proyectos. La visita nos gustó mucho ya que estábamos solos en el museo a diferencia de otros museos más masificados. Nos explicaron un montón de cosas sobre el mundo africano pues ofrecen una visita guiada muy completa e interesante. Además todos los expositorios nos ayudan a entender mejor como es la vida allí. Todo está muy bien cuidado. Es un museo adaptado para personas con movilidad reducida y cuenta con baños a la entrada del museo. ¡Repetiríamos sin dudarlo!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICDusjT0QE\u0026fid=0x0:0xb02e9a979e8218c4"],"When":"2024-3-27"},{"Name":"Fran Sanchez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWn-aYO4v5n5EpWlNE8_TvXHU9rQtrPrpBFTWQ4WOEiH1VU2Fxo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Es un sitio muy agradable, es pequeño y tranquilo y la responsable es muy buena explicando...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDuiyHZfeEvk4K5QsjRlgfo\u0026fid=0x0:0xb02e9a979e8218c4"],"When":"2026-1-28"}]

[{"Name":"G Mac","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfFY2KM7hfNlooonhvV8_u_Q6GiEzn9xdqejVwqehpMdOIKlCRug=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a fantastic building with some wonderful artworks. The ground floor has some student works many of true quality and talent. You descend one floor to see an exhibition of graphic arts from the early 1900s on. You descend to the main exhibition space which has a moving collection of work depiction the trauma of immigration and the victims of this dangerous journey via sea.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAKVHdLqOJeu4cq3_Ew79dC&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2025-9-24"},{"Name":"S Bristol","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI0zVxZqdB273yxQV1VinjzNJsA0sZ28LlFSrl4rEY9BlkzSg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An excellent museum in a lively neighborhood with many independent cafés, bars and restaurants. A history of the early Spanish illustrated magazine  ' White and Black ', showing many covers and early cartoons in chronological order from 1901. Varied artistic illustrations showcasing the ' modern life', politics etc. Other exhibitions on more floors. We visited towards the end of the day,  and could have easily spent 2 hours there, plus visiting the cafe. Recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDzmOedbw&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-6-7"},{"Name":"Marc G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK2hJP8h7COFWvZMrwQPcpLDair4JLlEimwAnddDETKQNGIvV5s=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved the Museum and the (free!) guided tour. Wonderful to go from 1890ish all the way to 2000 in history through caricature, illustration and art… don’t miss out on visiting this place! I can confirm that very young & upwards will enjoy this colourful and fun exhibition. Most pieces are driven by action & personality of the subjects, or by the relatable situation of the historic moment they were created in.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDTkKuJOQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2024-5-21"},{"Name":"Will Finley","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVE9RXbySJxSR7Ize1IBsmiQkteh52ZtMoGsP7LqIwsVeMif9Er=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Not so much a museum as it is a book store. Which by itself is amazing. It is the largest selection of design books I've seen in one place. Very interesting stuff from all over the world with beautiful design. The building is also fantastic. The metal panel facade is striking and the underlit floor modules are really cool looking at night. They gate off the courtyard when it gets dark though. The museum is not exceptionally inspiring, but you should still go up to the second level to walk through the connector.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDE_-TNHg&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2017-6-14"},{"Name":"Elisabetta Borzini","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWW9OaJFHYeOlmWeyc9v25DLabeFhCxN7jRlbMj8RkcjNuLAtMf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"So, the museum is actually closed but the exhibition on the ground floor is open (and free).
It’s an exhibition about transgender people, trans art and trans artists. I didn’t expect it and I’m so grateful we stumbled open it because it was so heartwarming and marvellous. Thank you for this space!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICFjay90wE&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2023-10-30"},{"Name":"Christian Jimenez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKJschGurXRAeIT0xo2IXQDdEoBMGg9nvZnG8j56pfgoYf7jg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Free Museum featuring Illustration work as well as some digital work. Reader be warned, everything is in Spanish.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID4loD5fQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2017-5-17"},{"Name":"Shea Gallagher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXo2wI3q1e5oC5KoDnAr91sqejKTG3sycFuqlF7lDZ1clIBeIg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It's pay what you think (3euros recommended which is well worth it) and features some amazing artists of various late 20th century fashion paintings/ designs and at the minute there is a what I would describe as geometric art on the first floor.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE_dXyMQ&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2019-3-16"},{"Name":"Mariia Lozben","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWL49abyt_wP-fLEYIyYb8q3sxUdKHVFjw4m1aatuvE2XcV99N8-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great place for inspiration 👍 there are an exhibition of illustrations on -1 floor and Madrid's railway history exhibition on 2 floor. Very interesting.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICc1vjNYg&fid=0x0:0xfa3f6bfad1262473"],"When":"2019-11-27"}]
