user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Raz Maoz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_JjPTGQF7fDVY4j9TjcAXUbfEf0FTyO37LPVvC3DZ87QKsidCeA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We visited this museum with different expectations and both left absolutely fascinated. For my wife, who has studied the history of fashion and haute couture, this place fit like a glove. She found a professional and deep analysis of silhouettes, textiles, and craftsmanship at the highest level.
But the real surprise was for me. As a history buff, I found a comprehensive and gripping chronological journey. The clothes are just the medium: through them, you see the Industrial Revolution, the wars, the political shifts in Spain, and the social transformations of Europe.

It’s far more than a 'costume exhibit' – it’s tangible history, phenomenally organized, from 18th-century corsets to the groundbreaking designs of Balenciaga.
A true hidden gem in Madrid, highly recommended even if you don't consider yourself a 'fashion person'.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I enjoyed this museum quite a lot. The descriptions were good for the pieces, and they were arranged in chronological order with historical context for each section. Some of the exhibits are dark -- which is by design. Light is extremely damaging to textiles, and the low light is necessary to preserve the items. The low light of the rooms is to prevent a harsh glare on the glass of the exhibits, making it easier to see the items in the low lighting that is required. The only thing I personally wish was included was more information about the people who made the garments, as well as the processes of making the materials & garments. It was a nice relaxing way to spend a Saturday afternoon.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCwoNnLZA&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-3-15"},{"Name":"yael fayna","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJG-0a9FbqzdCVfD7oKkmOCuuvZztmzAR4nBVqvWImted6o3w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It is not at the city center and this is the benefit, very interesting museum I loved the curatorial process in the sexhibition","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBOvLgHqXlu-cq6TWkzZ-q3&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Jakub M Ochab","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXWeOsgMckABLzCjQG-D7kanzxb_tm5gLRx84U6XGHKPXlPU4Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We visited the Museo del Traje again over the weekend, and it continues to be one of Madrid’s hidden gems. Despite its location on the edge of the city, it wasn’t very busy—which actually made for a peaceful and enjoyable experience. It’s a perfect option if you’re planning to spend time in nearby parks or looking for a quieter cultural stop away from the city center.

This time, we went specifically for the seasonal exhibitions, and admission was free. The exhibitions were well-curated and offered a fresh perspective on fashion and design. The museum staff were all very friendly and attentive, which added to the overall positive experience.

There’s also a nice café on site where you can relax with some food, coffee, or a drink—ideal for a break during your visit. Don’t miss the gift shop downstairs either. It has a great selection of fashion-related books and unique items that are definitely worth browsing.

All in all, the Museo del Traje is a wonderful blend of fashion, history, and calm atmosphere—definitely worth a visit whether you’re a fashion enthusiast or just looking for a quieter cultural outing in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCeMzq71dIhXAPbZQjIYB1X&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-7-20"},{"Name":"Alejandro Perez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKHk2nbyV1HYyUXx8bXnKtGbRG7n3r_5fqwa7ozq4iCVkJWQw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Small and amazing museum. Great place to bring your family!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB8aNK2UHUlAiv9nPBuWhWl&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Jelen Sita","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLahMROOG8JNp7RJvZFjIqI8kI5SuUrnqmMJd-MtC6n4zfMvQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The day was one of those frigid windy winter days in Madrid that chill you to the bone, especially if you walk there from the metro station- a good 10 mins away on wide avenues which only act as wind tunnels....When I arrived I was greeted by an impressive über-modern "container- looking" building - the only drawback was the equally impressive set of cement stairs that lead up to the main entrance(*).  I am sure they had a wheelchair accessible elevator from the ground up but I didn't have time nor the inclination to look for it!!
Now as a veritable human popsicle I was greeted, -after trudging up the stairs- by a large but well heated foyer (which was appreciated instantly). It was sweetly staffed by welcoming people who chuckled commiseratingly at my thankful antics.
It is an easy museum to navigate (all on one floor) and the collection here was far fuller (in regards to the male clothing) than the one in Barcelona. It is amazing how, as a society, the man has been pressured from wearing intricate and delicate embroidery with beautiful floral patterns to a stark simple dark clothing with no embellishment excepting, perhaps, the cut. The only nod to a more changeling offer for the guys was the modern and future clothing, which was interesting.
The female showcase was very prevalent throughout the ages and explains pretty well what the intricacy of the layers were. The most amazing is to consider the fact that the apparatus to enhance the "derrier" was ever worn...
Most of the clothing was representative of the high society so I really enjoyed the great collection of what the less "economically proud" wore- which shows far more useful logic in its design.
There is also a modest lineup (13?) of  clothing designed for the blind to be able to touch and get a feeling of the changes in clothing (mostly female, one male) during the ages. I found this wonderfully inclusive. They could offer a taped explanation (or on tablets in Braille?) of each piece (in the "sighted" part) for those that are recently blind but remember colors and textures?
I would have liked a bit more info of each: the materials used, the name of the designer (if known), the price it might have cost (translated to nowadays euros).  One thing that was commendable is that all the explanations, that were there, were translated into English.
I would suggest they look into using  l.e.d. lighting for those windows where light was very dim so not to damage the cloth...what is the use if you can't see it?!

Overall a great museum! Doable in one hour or so (since it has few placards), with a couple of didactic screens to explain some of the displays...

(*)Oh there is also a lovely looking cafe and a temporary collection (they were announcing "Jeans") both of which are at ground level but I didn't have the time to visit.
I highly recommend it (but dress -ha!- well if coming from the metro in winter time!)
P.S. I asked if they ever staged a real "runway"(pasarela) of modern designs (they could be students' creations?!) but they didn't understand my question and seemed eager to close...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID1gYWo2QE&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-1-7"},{"Name":"Seokjin Ham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhtf6A7X2UDP--Xi1vhwXxfhLJk0GUh_pTYJvgqrBOTATbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This is one of the national museums where you can explore the history of Spain's traditional clothing and fashion. The building was built in 1973, but the current museum opened in 2004. It houses more than 160,000 items of clothing, accessories, and textiles. Because it is relatively quiet, it is easy to obtain an annual membership to the national museums. There is a beautiful garden nearby, so you can enjoy a leisurely walk.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDP7JTNRQ&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-11-29"},{"Name":"Kathy Patton","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUI5xWFJ7mPNsJDqWmP_hMM6aI9XBZmZV1AQIcLLMEYEq0_zx4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such an interesting museum learning about Spanish history through clothing across the years. We really enjoyed our visit, especially on a hot day in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDrhYapjgE&fid=0x0:0x48d7713174738e82"],"When":"2024-7-18"}]

[{"Name":"Tom De Busscher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ-cAQEDImJ9213WVMDwLr4p9MIwbp_GetCsksfq4eFMuVYog=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A great free museum that is both informative as interactive. You can easily spend 2 hours here without getting bored. Very friendly staff also to help you on your way or guide you through the do it yourself experiments.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBOdoPX8OHWFKZxbsdmC0TP&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2026-2-10"},{"Name":"Murphy Tassin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVQ6HffsLHHWUMdbUvqs0Oz3J_5_npo-FR-6fL1t4ZwIBvM4Cyo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"🎉 Looking for a Fun Day Out with the Kids? 🧒🦕🔬
Discover the National Museum of Science and Technology – where learning meets adventure!

🐾 Explore hundreds of fascinating stuffed animals
📸 Snap sweet pics of animals that look like they're smiling
🧠 Hands-on exhibits that spark curiosity
🎨 Perfect for curious minds and family fun!

Smaller then I expected.
Bring the whole family for an unforgettable day of science, smiles, and discovery!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBIAqiw6JNcUJ6Byqx-92VI&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2025-7-12"},{"Name":"Toby Vanderbeek","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWlXJDQn2lV2nSAZdrNBVTf3y9WAh7oQy-ckbfCFTLEE5PuSacZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Entrance is free. There’s a small fee for the planetarium and some other experience. There weren’t too many people. It was a Thursday, early afternoon. The ground floor is a static exhibit. The kids didn’t care. We went up to the first floor to play with the interactive exhibits. This was much more fun. The kids played for about 30 minutes before one got bored and wanted to leave. I wanted to go into the planetarium but there was no consensus so we left. If your kids are curious, they will like this place. There’s enough to keep adults interested too.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID-r7ya3QE&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2022-12-12"},{"Name":"Natalia Crea","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXajgzA7hbow_GjIV6kMCEXh_S1w_LjmUhkMcIXCeobjSHj7Jw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved it! There's nothing better than an interactive museum where one can learn, play and have fun all in one!
Entrance is free which is also great.

There are some exhibitions and workshops that aren't free and also a planetarium which costs 3€.

I would recommend to get your tickets in advance if you are interested in visiting the planetarium as it does get full pretty quick, specially on the weekends.

If you are adults going by yourself and can avoid the weekends, I would say: avoid them! You are gonna get stuck behind lots of kids trying out the experiments and won't let you play as much as you want. So take care of your inner child and visit the museum during the week.

There are lockers to leave your belongings if you wish to, just be aware some keys don't have number tags so you will have to remember the number. It might sound obvious (we thought so too) but then we saw a few people trying to open every locker as they couldn't remember their number.

All in all: a great museum!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDNuK2DuQE&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2024-1-20"},{"Name":"Sergio Carvalho","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUZNLFi9DRa31g0Stayl7dv4VAPWzZSxjaxhD7M8IDe6Qj8quvsjg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Kids loved it! Some restrictions still apply due to covid which limits the options to interact with all experiments. Super friendly staff. Lockers available. Be there early. Get familiar with all offerings as they vary over time: Interactive tour, planetarium, temporary exhibits,...","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICWwe3UUg&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2022-2-28"},{"Name":"Jonathan Fisher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXt4LVCEhYpL-x9MmumU7fZ2R1nZnSnimbeFlrdtAWj0nE6toqR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We only had a short time but we could see that this needs about half a day to get a full sense of all the fun demonstrations and exhibits. They've done a great job of making the stories and physical effects of things accessible and interactive. The ramps and walks are fun to move around and give you time to tell your kid(s) more about the things they have seen and touched. If the museum still has the hanging metal curtains on the first floor when you visit though, they don't like if you mess with them ;)","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"vivek sinha","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUaCbHSdt14RuLdfrqca3_Ldhc6qjlErLkVWfIrvlWuX_DjBVBJCw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A must visit place ... plenty of things to do with science experiments

Entry fee - free for museum(though you have to take tickets) ; 3€ for planetarium

Have three floor - ground floor mostly old bike cars movie making and health care equipment’s

First floor is best part - have plenty of interesting things (explaining science concepts),  any kid would love this place

Top floor have planetarium

There is a cafeteria as well on ground floor

Overall, a must visit place and ideal for a half day trip","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE9dqnJw&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2018-3-27"},{"Name":"Anand Gothe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXqs2VkIj9tXRc5O7ejBKu9o9fYN-4R9uT1UuOyGVxqh6e5bhnEjA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome place, for kids and adults alike. Lot of interesting items and experiments on display. All the labels and descriptions are Spanish. You'll need about an hour to cover the two floors. Entry is free to both these floors. If you speak Spanish, I highly recommend the planetarium on third floor (for EUR 3). There is also a picnic section on ground floor for kids and families to enjoy their meals.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICk883bfw&fid=0x0:0xbe8383505998a789"],"When":"2018-5-31"}]

[{"Name":"Jaspal Singh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXKoK6lQQKruIpwS2QBDpwAj__9wnNd6RsyiWPI2xdMIy04VT-H=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"February 2026 5*

Visited the museum on Friday evening. I spent 3 hours and could not finish it half of it. This place is very amazing and beautiful collection. I really want to also shout out for staff who are excellent. I bought ticket online from GetYourGuide but did not get it. The staff issued me ticket on the spot.

There is big art collection from different era. There are 3 floors and covering different segments of different centuries. It is so interesting to see the collection. You can get audio tour from the museum.

One of the floor is closed which is covering Arabic art. You can see Hispanic and Roman arts.

The place is fully accessible and will need good time.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAn_Df4pAfgFNOFyHtdqjEX&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-21"},{"Name":"Sophie Macfarland","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKkDSyC73O6U7wn67SRhdMV7tLfHakEHRayagFeeQe5pM9JzA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This was a great stop!

The ticket is very cheap and the museum walks through Spanish history (starting with pre-history). The collection, while good, is not good enough to warrant a long visit. However, the signs and curation are phenomenal (English signage is included). The outline and explanation of history was great and I learned a ton. This was a perfect foundation for the rest of my trip to Spain, bouncing between Phoenician, Roman, Visigoithic, Islamic, and Catholic sites.

In particular the maps were amazing and I loved the late Roman mosaics.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA-lfZOKY30aOAJGmJQApKR&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-9"},{"Name":"Michael Offord","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUCYcnQ9p_fF7bpz5p0dlxJtJ-6yRZkgizWzoZieS9pO2nu0Dw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a quiet but very good museum detailing the early history of Spain, from pre-history to the Romans. There is also an excellent permanent exhibit on Egypt.

Some carvings are top quality. This is not the same size as the British Museum or the Louvre, but is very visitable and a notable collection of Roman emperor busts, though unlike many other former Roman provinces, Spain has not centralised their Roman archaeology, and much of it remains in regional museums.

Very easy to get in with no queue and extremely reasonably priced. Also modern facilities.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBDVxuKkhh1jH8Zit6CxnO-&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Chiahsun Liao","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV4TDJ6SlyonvuEbjhTra3qvN1QpGzvmehP9hkS97J4t9BvqKFH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A must-visit museum for history enthusiasts. The collection is highly informative and well presented throughout, covering major human civilizations across the historical timeline with impressive depth. It was surprising to see how lightly visited it was, given the world class quality of the exhibits. It is one of the few museums in Madrid that provides thorough and professional English explanations for every exhibit. The museum also offers very convenient amenities, including spacious restrooms on every floor and a self-help cloakroom that can fit in carry-on sized luggage, which can be a huge bonus for tourists. You can easily lose yourself in the river of history and spend an entire day exploring. A true hidden gem and, in my opinion, the best museum in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD8emR2DIgqvJHvvDaWlXcv&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"Daria Khenkina","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNRdxiUGq7jBgczsAL2sQjH8oqhoDG11AET9kNczYnHhWopYPx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the best museums I've been to. Rich and diverse collection, very beautiful artifacts. Definitely recommend. Spent here about 2 hours,   but would prefer to spend more","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDsJm2IPD_TBFrx2jpnu8I2&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-2-14"},{"Name":"Bob Meadow","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJU2pLYy4QH7AEa64d-1yxozoqEo9N-RU-m9HK-wQ0knCCn3g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Located next to the National Library, the National Museum of Archeology offers an amazing journey through the history of Spain from the Paleolithic to the 19th century. The collection includes artifacts dating back to prehistoric times, the Iberian peoples, Roman Hispania, the Visigothic kingdom, Al-Andalus, the Middle Ages, the Modern era and so on. On the other hand, the collection of the ancient Greek and Egyptian civilizations located on the second floor, is really enjoyable to explore thanks to the quality and quantity of the pieces. And all this for just 3€. Don't miss it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDbzyA8HM2TEYP5Hgye9jbx&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-18"},{"Name":"Elaine Huang","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLbBOdrqJ3ChpJY9ADZrIwE3kyztaS4mN7ayUXI_HJtKjf7PA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I participated a guided tour and Sophia gave me a fantastic experience. She shared a lot of information and unknown stories beyond audio guide (the app is really helpful as well). This museum gives me a good reason to come back to Madrid and I would love to stay in the museum whole day by then ~","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBvp-6TJ5VCxKHIGZM1JcP1&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-17"},{"Name":"Sandra Gregan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUAzKIkf4GjI17qG_PAe8kJGNxVGaF6ZI_6fNjp_9a6xaxwo508=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of my favorite experiences while in Madrid. Well thought out floor plan and displays. Easy to navigate and amazing exploration of history from the beginning of time. We purchased tickets online prior to entering. Very easy process and only €3/person which was a steal, I would have easily paid more. Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBQCFNJfjVT1IZuzPeu8Ejg&fid=0x0:0x3bdf2473c721c4cb"],"When":"2026-1-16"}]

[{"Name":"Sidónio Sousa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXkV_4xUfxZJNhW1rio2RuLNJ7VwEDjaWuJlHV3nHya9uXTSmw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"WOW! The interior is just magnific! A gem itself. Then you have a huge collection of so beautiful minerals and rocks. Not many tourists, more students so i really enjoyed the time here.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDQZd1Q4E5ra-XM9Cw7ndni&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2026-2-22"},{"Name":"Nay Nay","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJQCUewEak1WfsulkdX5J5auXOGaksWgBmPLrQWigfapxhpOQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The building is spectacular! I love the way they have the mineral set up very easy to go through. They have some beautiful local minerals as well as international minerals. You need a few hours to really enjoy it. Highly recommend for children. It’s a great learning experience into the world of geology.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCKywUvmGEprbH7wc1UrAAF&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-8-24"},{"Name":"Tundra Caoduro","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVI2Rm51a38V1f4cEnxrpoBjFpDziiLKdc5NnRN64BBZrhfnw8T=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free museum, really well kept and the collection is HUGE.
I love minerals and whenever I visit a new city I always go to Natural Science museums, because usually there are none dedicated only to minerals.
On the ground floor you can find fossils, minerals and rocks. 1rst floor is dedicated to fossils and the second floor displays minerals and rocks found in Spain, divided by each region.
The thing that I appreciated the most was that each mineral had it's own chemical composition written on the label, which was really cool and I've never found it before at any other museum I've visited!!
If minerals are your passion or you have a few hours to spare, you should definitely pay a visit!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAvmG6F7HzQ3IZaO0kMq2fs&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-8-26"},{"Name":"John Chan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKpohnOR1hE_wIXJZG3Ya9rbeQxjwThGDIa-38jsFRg_daS0w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Would give it 10 star this place is amazing! So much information and so many gems and fossils to see! And it was free!!! Don’t forget to bring your passport to enter.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC3jpIwe8k1ifZJRK7cXnVt&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-10-3"},{"Name":"Alexandra Dejanovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXH99b1546rvNCbgmNKFiM9BFjVK09XRBF_MD8gVEGaAodbUg4qEQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The museum is free, but you need to show your ID at the entrance. No reservation needed. The collection of minerals is impressive! The building itself is beautiful.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBPhPPZWEO5MBYbVoxHWFBS&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-11-19"},{"Name":"Stephanie C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX2JxsBQxBaL1s9YhQjw9gvuremUuIkgGLeibn5hNIRwz7kkMbm=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This museum was an absolute gem (pun intended). There were thousands of fossils, rocks, and bones to marvel at spread out over several floors that were accessible via spiral staircases. The space itself was stunning, with a large open ground floor (that had a mastodon skull on display!), glass sunroof, and exhibits around the periphery. The descriptions were almost all in Spanish, but even without the descriptions, the items on display were impressive. Definitely a must-see if you're in the area.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCI5svuZA&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-4-1"},{"Name":"Will Gayle","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV6RQl5zC19Zbiw5cAHGko5yk7r9GI5TwXDRmwSPrbhg_3Fbi0o=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Gem of a place to visit. It rocked.

The workshop for kids (in Spanish) is great and the building is beautiful.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAZxzNhvC266-Vc5p7T1PGD&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2025-12-13"},{"Name":"Jim McJim","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVx1SVrTzDK2VplleC5LqOhHnk6_nFtRO6kAW8Q8YFF_czS7QGm=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wow. Just wow.

We are hobbyist field collectors from UK and generally find mineral museums to be a bit of a jumbled mess, or missing the high quality systematic/locality based approach.

This museum was exceptional, great specimens of both fossils and minerals, lots of info and was free to enter. We spent a couple of hours and I could have spent more, but we were only visiting Madrid for the day.

Was great to see some specimens from our home turf, and one from a locality I have visited in the UK

Only gripe was the majority was not translated, but we can not expect this as visitors to another country.

Would highly recommend visiting!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICdydaD7wE&fid=0x0:0x7232a651cb9e9189"],"When":"2024-2-16"}]

I have visited many museums in Spain over the past 4 months. Some were special, like this one, some awful, especially those that put modern visual art inside the classical room's with their 4 white walls.
Neomudejar is an edgy old factory location ( I am pretty old so I am happy to admit I worked in factories like this) and well, the aging space compliments and brings out the art so much better.
The staff were real, chilled and smiling. A first given the amount of bored, sitting in the corner, flicking their phone attendant's, I have seen in most Spanish art museums.
I am still so delighted to visit honest spaces like La Neomudejar,
but realistic enough to understand that the sad "Instagram" generation will not get their self need hits in such a genuine art space.
By thanks and appreciation to the real artists who still appreciate originality and edge, over mundane copious copying","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCAiuOtJQ&fid=0x0:0xa9e4000c30a4d6a0"],"When":"2025-1-30"},{"Name":"RAVE","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXZkEtNCn047-jM4dPo37rcR69TYzKQQ9RPQ0ETwVOXUhBSVz89Sw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It was excellent, a small museum, with very interesting curating all inside an old factory. user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Michael Offord","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUCYcnQ9p_fF7bpz5p0dlxJtJ-6yRZkgizWzoZieS9pO2nu0Dw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a really beautiful place with an interesting family History. The Dukes of Alba have a long history in Spain and are still close to the monarchy. However, they are also Dukes of Berwick in Scotland, and claim descent from the Stuarts. There are many paintings of Stuart ancestors, including Henry VII, Mary Queen of Scots, Charles II and James II. The family moved to France during the Glorious revolution and served the French Monarchy before moving to Spain.

The state rooms are luxuriously decorated and the gardens are very beautifully presented.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBFiQ1B0fRXRTDr6zsZRScp&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-2-21"},{"Name":"Lucas Kovács","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUAilLNzd-8YrLSM-6EX9NLiSIkwgxKq6129nEafcsyV9U-HPsV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Best advice I can give you! Visit on Tuesday afternoon or Sunday afternoon — tickets are only €10 instead of the regular €22. Also, don’t miss the stables! Most people rush straight into the palace and completely skip them, but the visit starts there. Bonus tip: there are free tickets booking online on Mondays at 10AM. They are available one week in advance.

The palace was quite impressive, beautifully maintained and rich in history. It houses more than half of Christopher Columbus’ autograph documents — meaning original handwritten documents by Columbus himself, which is pretty remarkable.

Inside, it’s not just about paintings (though there are many, with references to several famous painters). You’ll also find original decorations, furniture, sculptures, everyday objects, and a large number of family portraits spanning generations, which really helps tell the story of the place over time.

I visited on a Tuesday afternoon, and it was not too crowded, making it easy to enjoy everything at a relaxed pace. The palace is closed on Mondays afternoon.

An audio guide is available in English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. There are 14 rooms plus the gardens included in the visit, which didn’t take more than an hour.

A note on the gardens: the French gardens were not fully open during my visit. They’re currently only visible from a balcony near the front entrance on the left side, which is a bit of a shame but still nice to see from above.

Practical details: lockers, a shop, and toilets are available near the stables. There’s no café on-site.

Overall, a rich, well-curated palace visit with excellent explanations and great value — especially if you catch the reduced-price slots. Highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCsvYw5fRn8xWi9Xi7MOJRZ&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-2-3"},{"Name":"David Le Hen","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIs6VMqQkeI5f0KchTSIRDqNEm803FMAwTTPKCaA4J-DKI9vQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful palace from the Alba family. Must see if you  go to Madrid. Fascinating family.
Very helpful staff. The rooms are very well preserved .
It was very quiet today which made it even better experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAsMWwwmt0QwDbS8KbJK5wc&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-1-12"},{"Name":"Francisco Mortágua","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX7sxvZJCTDiKD-D3AFqbg2AnCG54qTFAk8k5PUqPEL1D9i3GBS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"A nice palace with some interesting objects, paintings, and information about the Alba dukedom and family. However, while it’s understandable that funding is needed for its preservation, the visit doesn’t quite justify the €22 ticket per person. Not really recommended — there are much better options to visit in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB1g8T3lUiorQuy-oOSntbe&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-10-26"},{"Name":"Michelangelo Wegner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIKHfAfojH9PkxUzgYpV7xFhOXOpHV47i2KwQoGgddGKIM1ew=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Liria Palace is a neoclassical palace. It is the Madrid residence of the Dukes of Alba. A beautiful building and grounds. Allow time to explore the interior for the beautiful interior decorations and artwork.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD9N2WeHIvTWx2OrxM7n-hB&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2026-1-19"},{"Name":"Joanna M L Fearn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUOfKWToaG8brnoJqigVBfiG4fJXSTatoZ79fuBMUEzCGoYUZ4E=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I specifically came to Madrid, to see the artist Joana Vasconcelos exhibition at the Palacio de Liria as I admire her work and thought the exhibition looks amazing.

This was my first time in Madrid. The Palace isn't hard to find, I walked there from Temple of Debod, it was about 10 mins. You can't see anything from the entrance because of the trees, the entrance is small, you show your ticket then go through airport style security then access to the garden to the palace.

There is a small prefab style building to the left which has lockers, toilets and a small gift shop.

The palace is very impressive. I had a wonderful time, there is so much to see and look at. It is very ornate and rich in detail. You are allowed to take photos as long as you don't use flash photography.

The gardens weren't open, not sure why, you can only access front of the palace, not the back. Nevertheless it didn't detract from my visit. The exhibition was well put together, I could take in everything at my own pace without being rushed and it wasn't overcrowded.

Joana Vasconcelos work is fascinating and intruiging, from stiletto shoes made from stainless steel pans and lids to Crochet covered dogs and abstract ornate figures. There is detail everywhere you look.

This was definitely worth the short trip to Madrid from London. I had a wonderful time. (July, 2025)","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAPaa-z6oy_cyiRjC-HHR0G&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-8-16"},{"Name":"joanna esparza","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXsdRcH_GPPGNKN6XfLgaY1Z5zL7aEqkCPHvXTlk1pq3AsuzBjQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went as soon as they opened. That helped me not feel rushed to go thru the whole building and it was quiet inside. I recommend earbuds so you can listen to the audio guide they have available in English. It comes with a lot of great info for every room.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCEMVwqJkt9-LjBtfdPXdd6&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-9-28"},{"Name":"Jon Flynn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXg5z6yaTbyt8STy1CXUcErg67hyrjKhM-1tFse9DOxsX4A5jaV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The palace is stunning and with the current exhibition you are also allowed to take photos. BUT for 23 euros you are NOT allowed in the garden. This is unacceptable. nevermind my life was I refused to walk around the gardens, they are far more interesting than the odd exhibition they have at the moment. Therefore only 4 stars. The staff were charming.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD75eb5UyMBCwO3rwbyWB9Z&fid=0x0:0xfebe93bbb6832c4f"],"When":"2025-6-23"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Lucas Kovács","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUAilLNzd-8YrLSM-6EX9NLiSIkwgxKq6129nEafcsyV9U-HPsV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The visit was overall nice and interesting, but I left wanting a bit more.

When I went, there were not too many people — just a couple of groups at first, and once they left, it felt quite calm with only a few visitors around. The ticket price is accessible (€7), and there’s an audio guide available (needs mobile data/wifi), which helps give context to the collection. The total visit took me about one hour, though if you’re really into trains, you could easily spend two hours or more.

Inside, there are toilets, a small shop, and vending machines for coffee, which makes it convenient. The collection includes many RENFE trains that operated in Spain, mostly steam and electric locomotives, and they are very well maintained and preserved. It’s definitely impressive to see them up close.

That said, there were a couple of things I didn’t love. The miniature train section on the second floor felt a bit rushed — it lacks landscape and detailed scenery; it’s mostly just trains running. Also, this area is not accessible, which is worth noting.

More importantly, I felt the museum could be more interactive. In a museum, you expect some level of interaction, but here the opportunities are quite limited. You can’t enter the locomotives or carriages, and in some cases you can’t even properly look inside. That’s a bit of a shame. This isn’t a train storage facility — it’s a museum — and opening up more spaces would really help spark visitors’ interest.

I’m not saying it wasn’t impressive — it absolutely is — but I was left wanting more. Under the current conditions, I probably wouldn’t visit again. If they allowed access inside some of the locomotives and carriages, I would definitely reconsider and recommend it even more strongly.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDSNuPK7DTyAp6qtZjL7L39&fid=0x0:0xb46b68a535be221e"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"JR Suarez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV125tV-n_qcVxm3oiGFaOYghpi_ecYeHDOTmo-qTf2pPzlJmkr=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went during the Motores Market that they have generally the second weekend of each month: Mercado de motores. More than 20 exhibits. But the small model exhibits are awesome!!! Really cool and lots of history & info.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCVGyYhjRE2hy1smNC2IX_J&fid=0x0:0xb46b68a535be221e"],"When":"2025-11-30"},{"Name":"Peter Aba","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWmDSnfYqLdtS7DkhvNrshz-rA0e66W-XElxLUG7nvKhbY_rM9v=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Steam locos are overrepresented. The current temporary exhibition is called  'So many worlds in this one"   It runs until 24 January 2926. If in Madrid don't miss it. It is very special. The whole museum is special and should be on anyone's must see list.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBre-lJP_i0JevFp0RPNa2-&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2025-11-5"},{"Name":"Seokjin Ham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIhtf6A7X2UDP--Xi1vhwXxfhLJk0GUh_pTYJvgqrBOTATbUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This museum was founded in 1875 by Pedro González de Velasco and is recognized as Spain’s first museum dedicated specifically to anthropology. What a pleasant surprise, very well laid out and filled with great, many from Spain, artefacts. You end up with a great sense of Spain's long history. We loved the Moorish sections with the arches, tiles and an incredible ceiling display. I recommend adding this to your visit and with the entrance fee at 3 Euros it is a great bargain.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID76c3BYA&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2024-8-25"},{"Name":"Carl Flores","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV5-UvVuQJWolN1JyF00AcESD4pizDr4hTKETyJtFOFgYEXy_OPJA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I visited the museum on a Sunday 12/22/24 and admission was free. It normally is € 3 for general admission. A great place to view ancient history that one does not get to see in the USA. It was an honor and treat to see ancient history and artifacts in person. Plus you are allowed to take photos.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDv44rNBg&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2024-12-23"},{"Name":"david howells","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXv00dm90sqOsiG9tuO5orrJOqUEDS-SlSH6CFfk8dmCBLrELi0Jw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Nice museum spread over three floors with friendly staff and just a €3 entry fee. Nicely laid out but the content is quite basic. The exhibits are just informative generally. This is a high school level exhibition really but good enough to pass away an hour or so.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIChmqjJMw&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2023-2-10"},{"Name":"Bret Bowman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVGD8UU6n0pSe3DnnoMF9LV7M8K2xYwXPGsIE1BfmUYZUf70Ir6=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I arrived this morning right at about 11am and was let in free. But the cost of €3 (read in another review below) is will worth your time to see the exhibits on display here.

Wow! Spain has definitely made their historical mark on the world. Ground floor is Asia (mostly Philippines). First floor is Africa. Second floor is Americas. This museum is a great reminder how much influence Spain has had throughout the years of history following their glory years of exploration and colonization.

You can probably get through this museum within an hour if you really rush. Two hours is more appropriate to see and appreciate everything. I went through in about 90 minutes or so and really feel enlightened by everything I saw and read.

I snapped some pictures, but they don't really do justice, so make sure you go if you can carve out time. It's well worth it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID2mK-_Ug&fid=0x0:0x676b975595ff8099"],"When":"2022-5-5"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Melissa Abache","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUw0vLeJHVoNMLUgkFmakRU-w4pqdCkX4s1cy5_kSpRWinYVZq2CQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a great activity for families with children of all ages including toddlers. You should book in advance (it is free but they have limited capacity for the guided tour) and it is worth doing the guided tour to learn about very useful fire and safety information. The museum has several antique firetrucks, tools, displays and the guided tour finishes with a short animated movie for kids. It is only available in Spanish but still worth the visit if your toddler is obsessed with firefighters. To get there you should get down at the “Buenos Aires” metro stop on line 10 and then walk for about 7-8 min to the Museum. You will see a tall building with red letters that says MBM and that is it. Very friendly firefighters guide the tour and answer all questions. There is nowhere to have a drink or snack so come prepared but it has restrooms.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICBmcLiHg&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2022-12-29"},{"Name":"Darusik1","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLxCoDECInj0I-kF5XYPvejjQUPtQlQtc_Tl3-esghw6xmt4lQr=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Trying to get there two times and all the time huge dissapointment for the child as its always closed despite the announced hours. Please note that not only you can visit during week days (why by the way? Arent all the kids at school at this time?!🙄)  but even if you finally got a free day with your kiddo you will also need advance booking to get inside....otherwise you will be left behind the door dealing with severe child tantrum. Which is very sad as it looks like a nice musuem on the photoes, ans with the kid who is obsessed with fire trucks and all related things its sad that it is so complicated to get in:(","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"LUCIA DIEGO CARRIL","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXgEgWEPqN0vf01ue8zfJwglf_S0KhKvB-aKrQiWM0cLpxxn8_SLg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Es un museo que merece la pena visitar donde te cuentan la historia de los bomberos, los tipos de coches que han tenido que usar a lo largo de los tiempos y los tipos de maquinarias, además de que te enseñan algún vídeo explicativo. Es una visita de 1h que es gratuita. Pero recomiendo mucho ir, además es un sitio donde suelen ir con niños también. Es muy educativo.
Y al final de la visita te dejan probarte el casco con una chaqueta para poder hacerte una foto con ello puesto.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDu_hWBFi5BIsK5nmQvts8v&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2025-10-2"},{"Name":"Sergi Labernia Pérez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW16RKso-FkghmtROU9m0lvnut95mFjKWdfLrFS0qOHCW5ZO4CJ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Cal reservar. Molt bones explicacions encara que una mica llargues pels mes petits. Pels d'aproximadament 3 anys ens comenten que millor anar a una estació de bombers el dia de portes obertes. Al final de la visita els han deixat posar-se un casc i una jaqueta de bombers. En general el museu té peces molt interresants que expliquen la història dels bombers a Madrid i que són de molt valor. Moltes gràcies per la vostra feina i per la visita guiada.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCcYolnwavH7rjWM9F5W93I&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2026-1-12"},{"Name":"Carolina Da Costa","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWYfSgX0Lk0SuEctOO4gjXXttfiuDZHRunajNXXeWOSU0NTrhAj=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una visita muy recomendada, en especial  si vives en Madrid, un lugar lleno de historia, nadie se imaginaría los tesoros que tienen allí, me encantó. Debes reservar con antelación.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDKBFWY4eRUoN00FErjVd3x&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2026-1-28"},{"Name":"Swift Rivera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVi8Aupr9fkBY6kyosChZP4sFrnrEZNiLqXgIjgzQQbQ9NIWIk4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Si te gustan los bomberos y la historia, este museo es una joya oculta en Madrid. Tienen camiones antiguos, herramientas y equipos históricos que muestran cómo ha evolucionado el servicio a lo largo de los años. Las visitas son guiadas y te explican todo con mucho detalle. Es una experiencia entretenida y educativa, ideal para niños mayores de 8 años y adultos. Eso sí mejor reservar con antelación.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCQtKrLnwE&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2025-3-2"},{"Name":"El Glotón Gourmet","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWxUpfy9hbJ5n9HzXScwRGQinpNKocHoQbrOO8FjvHrl8qXiYCN=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo muy interesante sobre la historia del servicio de bomberos en Madrid. La visita guiada, realizada por bomberos, muy formativa","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBZe9TgpuDK24pRBeHOknqC&fid=0x0:0x570dcbfda52c110c"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Oscar Casas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKt9MiDg_GPchHd8LuExZFUExscCzSWRjIyK1pY-98a4pf2Yw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Visitamos el museo un fin de semana acompañados de nuestros hijos y fue una experiencia fantástica para todos. El museo está muy bien organizado y resulta especialmente interesante para los más pequeños, que pueden ver de cerca antiguos camiones, uniformes y materiales reales utilizados por los bomberos.

La visita es amena y educativa, ya que permite entender mejor la labor tan importante que realizan y la evolución del servicio a lo largo del tiempo. El personal fue muy amable y cercano con los niños, resolviendo dudas y haciendo la visita aún más entretenida. Un plan muy recomendable en Madrid para disfrutar en familia y aprender al mismo tiempo.","Images":null,"When":""}]

He would suggest to English speakers to take photos while he went over info and Spanish or the other way around d if he started in English. The trophy room
was impressive.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCqiO9WLLM-hhIYh5rHTipW&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2025-8-27"},{"Name":"Tom Hsu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU1flMM5VFFxDSBKboTZO8YX9JL5yC3NVROztkw1LxTJpvnyqOP=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Warning first: no bathrooom on a July weekday afternoon. You have to go out to a restaurant outside to get a bathroom after paying for a drink. Everything else is closed. The staff was not helpful. They could only say go outside from the atleti store and left or right. For that, I removed two stars. I had to endure taking my young ones outside in 95 plus heat for 15 minutes to find one.

Otherwise, I find the museum entertaining. We spent one hour there before the four o’clock tour began. I paid for basic which did not include the audio guide. However I enjoyed seeing the locker room, presidential section, and players’ entrance.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC6YcfXlCuFc0kdBsJL2Zdj&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Arjun Ghattaura","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVahdn9od80h24TDjAg4zt6Chpuf4BRnKiIjEjs-cehAKeYuuOI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We chose the Atlético Madrid tour and were impressed. The Metropolitano stadium tour gives you incredible access to the stadium, letting you experience what it's like to be a player. We saw the press box, changing rooms, players' tunnel, and even the presidential suite. The museum was also a great addition to the tour.
We visited midweek around noon, which was a great tip as it wasn't crowded at all. The staff were friendly and let us explore at our own pace. The stadium is new and impressive, with excellent facilities. It's easy to get to via the Metro, and the short journey outside the city center is well worth it.

Highly recommend this over any other stadium tour in Madrid due to the access to different areas in the stadium.

Tip - recommend to purchase tickets online before. This will let you skip any queue and go straight into the tour entrance.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAk0FUp61UOr8CyKe3SKE-M&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2025-8-5"},{"Name":"Thais Cannell","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLTs5vhy2yVVfQX7euGiI94f4kOXWO3ibC0_tugwcAxKAo22w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Lourdes was an amazing tour guide ! It is well worth a visit. The museum is excellent with numerous activities for all ages. The staff were excellent, very helpful and informative. One can feel the passion for this club. The multilingual tour guide, Andrés, was very knowledgeable and answered our many questions. Thank you tour staff for making this such a memorable time. Much appreciated.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICznLTKFQ&fid=0x0:0x79fe50ba6b4139dc"],"When":"2024-5-31"},{"Name":"David A","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVN8wd7C-7B9UolsIaQQwFGqL7Rgm7FUl1GZDRn54lTJvUqIDAcCQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Just done the guided tour and it was great. Bonus points to the reception for charging my phone for me as it was nearly out of battery!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Roger Urech","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWCXv2_eotkXkT4FzIedabV5uvwuaPF8zliHLhZ38ZIDNTunAY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"It was a fantastic experience, and I highly recommend the Atleti tour and the museum. It was a truly wonderful experience that Adrian made possible, so thank you again 😊","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Daniel Alqsous","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKw4rPao_9b4IaexHZnv92NM7flFJ-AguwiAkwBhOkX2SRiRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wonderful museum, with a lot of model ships and stories with most of them. You can find replicas, and true models, as well as submissions for competitions of rebuilding ships all in one place.

There were paintings too, and modern parts of the museum, with modern Spanish warships and different American ones too.

Overall very cool, would recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBNuEqbar0siUdLlhcK9EGS&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-1-19"},{"Name":"S T","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKf0nGKhvAcyMr52ndpaeM58FSuKNt5zegzQZSkqWHoe_20=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Voluntary admission well spend. Great museum, 100+ years old, amazing models, some of them large (3 meters long), lot of naval items. You can go from 14th century to modern era.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAI9BF7K7cMO4WUMQU-H5wJ&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-2-2"},{"Name":"Rae Zyon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUG1QtdDeQPQLETzpCBgyDyGAAEvIdbUpAe1YDVy3QwmQLrwpSA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Naval Museum in Madrid is a great place to visit. Entry is free, a beautiful museum preserving history. The museum's got the history of the Spanish. Loads of cool old stuff on display, all laid out really clearly so easy to follow.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBExZULt-3HCI6cxndrKaK2&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-1-6"},{"Name":"Naveen Perera","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXo1EOUBZmqNnX9TxAh0YA9pddlpk1dAqohyuijF1dv5BR7eDNB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very impressive small museum that clearly presents the naval history of Spain. Entry is free, which makes it a great stop even if you have limited time. The museum has multiple detailed models of historic ships, maps, and naval instruments that give a good picture of how Spain became a major maritime power.
There is a nice souvenir shop, but no café, so plan accordingly. The museum is well organised and easy to walk through, and you can comfortably visit it in about 45–60 minutes.
Overall, a worthwhile and informative visit, especially if you are interested in history, exploration, or ship models.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAVglefhROliQ0VvDWRhDkC&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-12-24"},{"Name":"Linda Bailey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV13-ZLd5E8gbX7oVINRLkobXoW7aOrzbAdXFra4V8v2e0Q96I=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Naval Museum is located in a gorgeous building with an amazing staircase leading to the museum. Entrance is free with a suggestion donation of 3 euros. There are no lockers and backpacks are not allowed.

The is an audioguide available to use on your phone. Well curated, lovely displays and most placard translated in English describe the incredible history of the Spanish Navy. A must see if interested in all things naval and Spanish  history.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD7vsS9qt10cynH-8O4MnjY&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-9-28"},{"Name":"Denisa Boar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUz4j1MccDX5a9w1bCEZ9L9NF0O4fR0gp_RcR6pqDA-cwFMYfY2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great museum for passionates and noobies. There is something for everyone: from navigation instruments and tools, to boats, accessories, equipments and uniforms, flags, guns, and fabulous paintings.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCH0O4RAFE3SHslWcmA_aCE&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2026-1-1"},{"Name":"Jeremy Lim","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUrc1Ud0khZyXU0C0tbctyuaBLhUoJQiepCDtbzj8M2mEPjfP8i=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Free museum with beautiful ship models and detailed history of the Spanish navy and the journey to the new world. With a visit if you’re interested. Takes about 45min-1hr to walk through.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCU5xl-OCuKupPM3RHkOjbq&fid=0x0:0x1d4c93520b9b0b36"],"When":"2025-9-8"},{"Name":"Ctgb2","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocItC9iDa9r9g_651odkZll6vFcFOS4APKRO34THBDIzmKIUQQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Naval Museum in Madrid feels like a hidden historical archive rather than a crowded tourist attraction. Located along the Paseo del Prado, it’s easy to overlook — but once inside, you realize it contains centuries of exploration, warfare, and global expansion.

The highlight is the collection of ship models. They’re incredibly detailed, almost architectural in precision, and they help you understand how naval technology evolved over time. You don’t just read history — you see engineering progress in miniature form.

One of the most fascinating pieces is the early world map attributed to Juan de la Cosa. Seeing the Americas represented in one of the first European maps gives a powerful sense of how people once understood the world.

What I appreciated most was the atmosphere. The museum is quiet and organized chronologically, making it easy to follow Spain’s maritime story from exploration to empire to modern navy. It feels educational without being overwhelming.

Overall, the Naval Museum rewards curiosity. It’s not flashy, but it’s deeply informative — a place where history unfolds patiently rather than dramatically.","Images":null,"When":""}]

La selección de temas es variada, abarcando diferentes periodos y artistas, lo que permite tener una visión general de distintas corrientes.

La organización de las salas es funcional y el recorrido está estructurado de una manera lógica. Sin embargo, en ocasiones, la cantidad de visitantes puede dificultar la observación detallada de las obras, especialmente en las muestras más populares, por lo que se recomienda comprar la entrada con antelación para asegurar el acceso en la hora deseada.

La información que acompaña a las obras es adecuada, aunque a veces podría ser más extensa para proporcionar un contexto más profundo. En general, es una opción a considerar para complementar la visita a la colección permanente.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marga Corchero","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVlWyxEwOZiFHq03iR3sWkk2iGO95iBcTWB8VMHtbu7Y-PoexzTYg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Maravillosa exposición de Gabrielle Münter. [{"Name":"Frankie Said","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU2J5NW-errqnDhcVVx-9IjO6uelZ4NwhNAbh1cFyVEiXqfjgpD=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing showcase of old metro trains, it is also free to visit with a valid ticket!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBPiPoi6YXXr5eWe_wYS2Sk&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2025-12-9"},{"Name":"M Jones","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVmJwIo0JukAFUEW1bYnsG_mzWp9ZqEKaH4p1YsaoAOTVtp40yZ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A fascinating glimpse of the past with the original Metro carriages arranged for a viewing, though it’s a shame that you can’t go into at least one of them, to feel the atmosphere. Behind the carriages are items such as doorhandles to switching gear, arranged in viewing cases.
TIP: if arriving by the Metro, don’t leave by the barriers to go to the upper concourse, because the Metro museum is at the level of these barriers on the left-hand side. If you go up to the top concourse, as we did, very small signs point back the way you came and we spent 20 minutes going back-and-forth until we finally found somebody to take us back through the barriers and point to the entrance of the museum. It really is very poorly signposted.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDFyIX5hwE&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2023-11-4"},{"Name":"Tomas","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWG4N_xEANm6FTsCjmbhpXPZnJVFWLrRkd_JtGvw59x8QrXquQn=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Great place, where you can see some old metro 🚇 vehicles in a very good shape.
Also in this exhibition it is possible to get information about the history of the Madrid metro 🚇.
And all of that without paying any entrance fee.
Sadly, you can't go inside these vehicles 😔.
There are staff on site, who can answer any questions if needed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDF4_evAw&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2023-11-9"},{"Name":"Manuel Gonzalez Vazquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW7DEtHZo8-9cH9OphhBsTFwF_pw91tcJyp1d4OGjmYWJ_mo1KK2w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The Museo Metro Chamartín is a space where you can discover the history of the Madrid Metro through an exhibition of classic trains and informative panels. It's one of the five historical spaces of the metro system and offers a glimpse into the past of this important mode of transport in Madrid
Access: Advance reservation is required for guided tours and recommended for self-guided visits.
Price: Free admission with a transport ticket.
The tour guide was really nice a made the experience both interesting and fun","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Tim S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVir8Qy9hVhBpfrpURplKPzjOC1VCr_-PPL7_fUBNM9OpIruLEOhg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"There's not a great deal here apart from old carriages but it's a good way to kill time while waiting for a train. All written info is Spanish only... As usual, the hours in Google Maps are wrong - but it seems they only open when they feel like it... It's free too.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICRsbreiwE&fid=0x0:0xa06a42c523ec7c7d"],"When":"2023-4-4"},{"Name":"David Antonio","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJXivTlIJSns9xqV01C0nu6oPEAkd2AsJrIRwscYBd94ryIew=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The tickets are free for students under 25 years old and senior citizens. The gift shop was full of great options.
Unfortunately the cafe is closed.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carlos Guerra","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXhX_SlrrlJrPxHz3M4ATPCqfsOVo-PJG3HC9pfpzkg-croDzDl=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very informative and entertaining. Fully accessible tactile museum with phenomenal models of architectural and historic items. Wheelchair accessible. Braille throughout. Free entry","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEOfgy57l_dvo5wE\u0026fid=0x0:0xd2de287a4d9f59b4"],"When":"2025-5-20"},{"Name":"thisnightsrevels","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWSA_SAxaLTpnurR5dA6Q_ETvzhMoDOwyyfrZXR_VjhofzMgRRP=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely fantastic visit and a definite highlight of my trip - everything from the building to the exhibits to the staff was so lovely and enjoyable. Highly highly recommend the visit! I'm autistic and have mobility difficulties and often find museums and galleries to be exhausting but this whole space is so relaxing to be in, you don't have to worry about harsh lighting or narrow spaces, you can just enjoy your experience ♥️ if I could give more than five stars I would ♥️ ♥️","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"José Viana","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVeMY03guRl8dRhHqeD9RML3CMvhvWz4hjQamvPAHur02N7lmiRkg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A really nice place with interesting things with support for the visually impaired. [{"Name":"Mykhaylo Kolesnyk","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWFxr6VV0hEaMOvzoeA3tlgpu5DwtnE94GKi_X3fayQ9qLcE1-w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Here’s the English translation:

⸻

I’m not sure whether all jamón museums are the same, but I wouldn’t recommend visiting this one. It’s possible there are better dishes, but out of the 7 dishes we tried, there was hardly anything truly enjoyable. I tend to think this is a very touristy place, since the dishes were brought out literally 2–5 minutes after ordering, which is kind of a plus, but the quality is a minus. There are much better places where you can spend the same amount of money (on average €30–40 per person) and get what you expect, or at least something close to it.

From the menu, we had:
•	Paella – it looked good and was quite good.
•	Grilled salmon, served with a salad of lettuce, carrots, and corn. The salad itself was okay, if not for the expired vegetables.
•	Pork tenderloin, served with French fries. Overall, not bad.
•	Caesar salad didn’t really resemble a Caesar salad, including the dressing. Again, the vegetables were a bit tired.
•	Jamón with melon. A separate story — the melon was cut into cubes and sprinkled with finely chopped jamón (not thinly sliced, but finely chopped like onion for meatballs). It’s served in an ice cream bowl. For the €11 this costs, you could buy 300 g of jamón at a supermarket plus a melon and eat your fill.
•	Bone broth. I tried it for the first time, but if you don’t want to experience a “pork kingdom” in your mouth, try something else.
•	Ice cream. Instead of butter, milk, and sugar, it felt like plastic and water were probably the main ingredients.
•	The wine was good.
•	The bread served with single-use packages of olive oil and vinegar was tasty. I’ve never seen oil and vinegar served this way before.

In February, when we were here, it was most likely off-season and there were few tourists. Whether that’s the reason or not is hard to say. The last time we visited this same museum, everything was 3–4 times better. This time, my wife refused to eat the melon and didn’t even look at the broth. And even I, being someone who eats almost anything, was chasing it with bread after just two spoonfuls.

Maybe next time we’ll try the assortment of 5–6 types of jamón, which costs around €34.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCkBZ0TpZ0nxrIpVfse64Um&fid=0x0:0xe3b77b0c922a34c3"],"When":"2026-2-7"},{"Name":"Kristie Park","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUFHqp730XGmbR5YV-LN7QvDrr1YvJlS45e8V5I8RvP0dgXIg8=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Museo del Jamón is a charming little place that's perfect for a casual stop. The bar has a great atmosphere – lively, no-frills, and very typically Spanish. It's a fun spot to sit, have a drink, and soak in the local vibe.

The food is decent – nothing too fancy, but it hits the spot if you're after something simple and traditional. The real highlight is the bar itself: quick service, affordable prices, and a wide selection of jamón and cold beers.

Overall, it's a great little bar for a relaxed break. Don’t expect fine dining, but do expect a solid experience in a very local setting.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhB17U6R5-YgbXCIhvzw1w-M&fid=0x0:0xe3b77b0c922a34c3"],"When":"2025-9-7"},{"Name":"Pusher Pushkin (PusherPushkin)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWpohyFWetY5qV5vANpyjlOC50iNbB9atVJ_3FA6R7ecKuc-xs=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had an amazing stay. There is a pretty nice dine-in area upstairs. The food was great and to our surprise not overpriced. Even tho it was very busy the Service was attentive and the food came quite fast. Definitely recommend it for a Madrid trip.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDi9lvNM4sS0iBcv4Sa1BKI&fid=0x0:0xe3b77b0c922a34c3"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Reyhaneh Askari","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXEhp3QZKl3Ngox6mFb8gwV7wvkFLyGsezdWd2p7aN6jRSo1X5z=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The experience was okay, I wouldn’t say that I was impressed or disappointed going there. The food was okay, nothing out of usual. We tried the Jamons, cheese and tortilla; they were good but not exceptional.
I liked the vibe and the history of the place rather than the food. I’d suggest going there for a short stop","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoSqbDLKHTvFGuQaHx2SYN&fid=0x0:0xe3b77b0c922a34c3"],"When":"2025-10-26"},{"Name":"J • B •","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXBWAWO7EoBBo7W_Sj0EoHtDptmWPPmQXFh3GUZ8Q8-98Oul6Ui=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo del Jamón on Calle Mayor is chaotic perfection and a touristy Madrid experience. Jamón legs hanging like art, beer flowing fast and bartenders shouting orders with charm. A caña and a plate of jamón ibérico here feel timeless and unapologetically local 😂. It’s not fancy, it’s real and that’s the point. Anyways, fun even for dropping by and just seeing it.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhD_ReOqYSKCXgikyP7pJNXV&fid=0x0:0xe3b77b0c922a34c3"],"When":"2025-10-17"},{"Name":"colin Harris","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUfjUl9BjvHdnlpWz4UoTgDV9_vnlFATqP1PFJbIdcriXCWHOc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"We visited the Museo del jamon located in Calle Mejor 7, near Plaza del Sol Madrid. After a long day walking we wanted to sit down and relax for a few minutes with a snack and drink. When we entered the colleague was really welcoming and friendly and stated we could eat upstairs which is the designated area for eating a proper meal not a quick snack. The waitress upstairs who served us queried why we were upstairs for just a snack and drink. She was not friendly and welcoming despite the fact we offered to go downstairs or eat and pay quickly. We ordered Patatas Bravas which when arrived was smothered with mayonnaise? not the traditional way to present this dish. We had it changed,which was fine and the replacement dishand drinks were good. Another couple were allowed upstairs for a snack like us and the same waitress was not happy with them either and appeared to upset them too. VERY BAD. The issue here is 1, the restaurant wasn't busy, so we were giving them business, 2 it is not our fault that we were sent to eat in the restaurant area by another colleague. 3 customers should always be treated friendly and respectfully and be made to feel value. There is plenty of other  dining options in this highly competitive area if Madrid","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Ferhat Yildiz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWV6W2gfwUqAFIDgffx1HATO6JsIPotrLYKf7X6lAbflWpp-7tw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I stopped by Museo del Jamón for a quick bite and the food was quite good — I had a croissant with cheese and jamón along with a cold beer, and everything was fresh and tasty, perfect for a light snack. However, the service was a bit disappointing. The staff barely communicated, offered no guidance, and there was no clear explanation on where to sit or how the process worked — you basically have to figure it all out on your own.

It’s a nice place if you’re just there for the food and don’t mind a self-serve vibe, but don’t expect much in terms of service or hospitality.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCo-a2sPw&fid=0x0:0xe3b77b0c922a34c3"],"When":"2025-4-18"},{"Name":"Marcus","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIZY2VcWRnrqCWyZy7NgSqAZsEzbS9knJDRqqAv9Et4HGt4Mg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Service was very fast. I ordered one plate of the best jamón ham and spent 16 euros for it. Not too bad. Taste was succulent.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAfb_WWufn3loM3hN9clPtv&fid=0x0:0xe3b77b0c922a34c3"],"When":"2025-8-13"}]

Está justo donde el ascensor, pudiéndose acceder desde la calle directamente. Se trata de una exposición con unos cuantos paneles en la pared de la historia del metro y de la estación de gran vía, con carteles informativos y objetos encontrados durante la construcción, así como algunas cosas históricas del metro. Está bastante chulo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCAkcP-Rg&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2025-2-1"},{"Name":"Aran A.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXfH4gTdkqeLJoNdPTUdr4gPj6MGWSVPQd-CSuAwybErQs_eJQxFQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"No hace falta pagar billete para verlo son unas tres o cuatro vitrinas donde se pueden ver cosas encontradas y antiguas del metro. Depende de lo interesado que estés pero se ve en menos de 5 minutos. Lo más interesante para mí es el emblema- escudo de Madrid","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDRy8uJKA&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2023-5-3"},{"Name":"Jordi Fajula","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJbvMkNWXEHot-zzw0mI3KEF1Ch8NJprx_I6RSrSHOzsdoFvA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Me ha parecido una forma interesante de conocer cómo fue una parte de la reforma de lo que es hoy Gran Vía. Puedes ver un emblema de madrid","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Raúl Fernández López","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX607swNaQjjzE_mhMfXHDElPsOGK5mKK6UpWfARpRFuleEVsKylg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Una parte importante de la historia de Madrid es la historia del metro de Madrid.

Metro a través de este pequeñísimo museo muestra todo lo que se encontraron tras la remodelación de la estación de metro de Gran Vía. De los restos a destacar fue el emblema de Madrid.

Museo de entrada gratuita ya que se sitúa fuera de los tornos del metro y se halla en la segunda planta.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDW8p7fHA&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2022-3-20"},{"Name":"Jörge","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXeWEXqdr5t3OlbRokb_Dhsg38mDGwuZo9JWUkx-spF0MuHs0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo en el que se exponen diferentes piezas procedentes de las excavaciones arqueológicas realizadas en el año 2019 durante las obras de ampliación y remodelación de la estación. Entre ellas se encuentran restos del antiguo ascensor que daba acceso al metro, escalones con publicidad, un escudo de la ciudad, y numerosos enseres y objetos cotidianos con más de cien años","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICRy7OQOw&fid=0x0:0x2a338d8296f82b7d"],"When":"2023-4-10"}]
