title: Bonsai Museum
address: Av. Olímpica, S/N, 28100 Alcobendas, Madrid
open_hours: {"Friday":["10 am–2 pm","4–6 pm"],"Monday":["Closed"],"Saturday":["10 am–2 pm","4–6 pm"],"Sunday":["10 am–2 pm"],"Thursday":["10 am–2 pm"],"Tuesday":["10 am–2 pm"],"Wednesday":["10 am–2 pm"]}
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Alessio Garau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWXwgwVSUnu7LxBPdgWtjTLnLD__Hy6lgiLJJjnhwE2uZgH1fy7=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently had the pleasure of visiting this fantastic spot just a short journey outside Madrid. Easily accessible from the city center for just a few euros, it's like stepping into a serene oasis. The attention to detail in maintaining the space is remarkable. Everything from the layout to the cleanliness is meticulously cared for, creating a truly tranquil atmosphere.

These aren't just ordinary bonsai; they're exceptional, unique pieces, some even centuries old! It's a rare opportunity to see such an array of timeless beauty.

I recommend visiting early in the morning. Not only is it quieter with fewer visitors, but the light at this time is absolutely magical, casting a serene glow over the gardens. It's the perfect time to fully appreciate the tranquility of the place.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICNkYmHvgE&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2024-1-15"},{"Name":"Patrick B.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUoL1DEfK7Bu7_hoNAjXKq5lk_LUu5npisoflWy_A8-MHsmHMY=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"This is definitely the best bonsai museum that I've seen so far. It's not huge but if you really appreciate bonsais then you'll spend 30-45min just appreciating the beauty of the bonsais. The entrance costs 3€ which is not bad in my opinion. It's also a great place to relax and I'm sure that if you take a book with you or you like to draw natural scenes then it's a very zen place to do so. It's obviously not in a centric area, meaning that is not close to the center or touristic area of Madrid, but if you have the means to go, then it's a good stop. But don't go out of your way just to go there, you can see bonsais at the botanical garden right next to the Prado museum. All in all a great stop to relax and enjoy an afternoon stroll.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICEqeW4jwE&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2019-5-21"},{"Name":"Iacopo Breschi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU1MxgP3bvgW9bEUwxoliISDL87kM367xoxfdO2JCa1rsy-yVtU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One amazing place.
For me the highlight of the museum, except from the tree themselves of course, is the arrangement. Each tree is displayed on top of stones or aged wood, against neutral yet rich of fines details backgrounds. White gravel of different sizes and reddish soil on the ground.
The contrast with the green of the plants is superbe.
The staff is very kind and helpful. I will come back for a guided tour.
The shop is small yet super cute with the mini plant compositions.
Thanks to all the people that work there to keep this place in such well maintained conditions.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICOwrfRtwE&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2022-6-5"},{"Name":"joshua gorr","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIwaLNGKXvnGdwuOR_qELDMVEuhYaSjIJpzg5WC8aQas1BErw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing collection of beautiful trees. Definitely a great place to check out.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Emaelia Norman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL0jJnJyhaFhUZPzuRonQXKLpLV2kllpoQYAAvj49XtYzU4eUM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"It's a nice little quiet place to visit if you're looking for something off the beaten path.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID429qaag&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2018-11-9"},{"Name":"Will B","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJlIc2yUz7rxrArXYCudvgc_BgeWKv0h0rIgRObj5bz4XX2qA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Loved, the selection of trees was very impressive","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICFncPxJg&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2023-10-31"},{"Name":"Almudena Navarro Sánchez-Mayoral","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUPQYaZCyXNuqF6ENdb6CX5B-lTGWo62v0TIj4mCFUV5_irX3s_=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An incredibly inspiring collection in the most beautifully designed garden. We had the luck of meeting Luis and briefly speaking to him, which made the visit extra memorable.
We will be coming back in Autumn to see how the trees change with the seasons.
Totally recommended!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"steven sedlik","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXvXvVl8Jd5dVaXWIEz0N9mYT8-Hw7KulSFOuAScfWYGByFsSuuHw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The attention to detail was amazing!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICP5f-3uQE&fid=0x0:0x1a5b05a65963fe4b"],"When":"2024-11-26"}]

It's less than a ten minute walk from Chamartín station and we used Google Maps to find our way there. On arrival there's an App you can download and can listen to all the info inside the museum in English (possibly other languages too). You can enter two of the buses inside the exhibition. All in all you're there for around an hour and it's good value for money.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDF94jDNA&fid=0x0:0x805d8dabc5d1cdd7"],"When":"2023-11-11"},{"Name":"Manuel Gonzalez Vazquez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW7DEtHZo8-9cH9OphhBsTFwF_pw91tcJyp1d4OGjmYWJ_mo1KK2w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba7-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"The Museo de la EMT in Madrid is dedicated to the history of the city's municipal transport company. It features a collection of vintage buses and offers virtual visits and audio guides. The museum is open on weekends.
It is a nice collection of the different models that were seen in Madrid over the years as well as maintenance and operational material
For people like me, it is a nostalgic journey back in time to some of the coaches we rode as children.
You need to buy your tickets in advance on the museum's EMT website

Be aware that the museum only opens on weekends during some months. For 2025, the opening times are:

Opening calendar for 2025
Every Saturday, Sunday and public holiday from March 1 to July 13 and from September 6 to November 16
Visiting hours: 10:00 – 11:30 – 13:00 – 17:00 hours.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Wai Kin Vinson Kong","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJxRNLlIIivUE1fHYHzwjU5pINcTjC85kcQ1nBwVdVzAEearttB=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I went to the museum without buying a ticket beforehand. The overall experience was extremely unfriendly for tourists, and I left feeling greatly disappointed.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICToofKbQ&fid=0x0:0x805d8dabc5d1cdd7"],"When":"2024-5-12"},{"Name":"Yaraslau Zauharodni","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLKgK_E46dUv9VJihj1cdxhKAHIlLFGMzgRUu8v7oRb9kAAWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"It was closed on Wednesday. Al entrar, uno tiene la sensación de estar viajando en el tiempo: vitrinas de madera, iluminación tenue y una cuidada disposición de las piezas crean una atmósfera que combina lo académico con lo casi museístico-artístico.

El recorrido comienza con modelos anatómicos de cera del siglo XIX, que impresionan tanto por su realismo como por el detalle minucioso con el que están elaborados. Estas figuras, muchas de ellas creadas con fines didácticos, muestran órganos, sistemas musculares y estructuras óseas con una precisión que todavía hoy resulta impactante. También hay esqueletos humanos, preparaciones en formol y una colección destacada de cráneos y fetos que permite entender la evolución de los estudios anatómicos en España.

Uno de los aspectos que más me llamó la atención fue la combinación de ciencia y arte. Algunas piezas, especialmente los modelos más antiguos, no solo buscan enseñar sino también provocar una reflexión estética sobre el cuerpo humano. Además, el personal del museo —en su mayoría estudiantes o profesionales de la medicina— está muy bien informado y dispuesto a responder preguntas, lo que enriquece mucho la visita.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCvhHDugJq2jC6CbaKS7s3z&fid=0x0:0x5e4509e9f7642276"],"When":"2025-8-29"},{"Name":"Mases Vinyard","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV29zDXlwWsdYYeFCr5CoLkafJ_J4G5h7UjAIk82-0UqbQYcuQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Estupena visita durante el jueves 06 de febrero de 2025.

El profesor Fermín recibió al grupo con entusiasmo, compartiendo con nosotros la historia y secretos del Museo y las espectaculares piezas que alberga.

Una visita para recordar siempre. Mis felicitaciones al profesor por su amabilidad y su gran labor didáctica.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Maite Leon-Sotelo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXmODoiCNxu0jg_-IlvyUX-QC9SnouE30EI88Di02L21Ei4ZvI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Es otra de las joyas que alberga la UCM, esperemos que no se pierda","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Aleksei Nasedkin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIDGogfOsA5W-1zde8PriymSPslyyAJGSnLC5og36t4XGCahQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Este Museo es espectacular. Especialmente estara interesante para personas quien quiere saber mas sibre historia de desarolllo de medicina, autopcia, patalogias de enfermedades. El doctor Fermin quien guia la excurcion es un gran especialista, cuenta muy interesante y siempre esta preparado a responder a cualquier pregunta. La exposición tambien es vale la pena a ver. La mayoria de los exponates no son de verdad, no son partes de cadaveres. Pero esas maquetes estan hecho de cera y tallados con mucha finidad. Admas estan exibidos mesa de autipsia de siglo 18, aparato de exibicion de cadaver de siglo 19, esquletos con patologias. Recomiendado 100% para visita.","Images":null,"When":""}]

Ahora mismo se encuentro en una sala muy grande, llena de paneles explicativos y vídeos.
Esta justo antes de entrar a la cafetería
La visita guiada está genial, la guía lo explico todo con mucho detalle y de una forma muy amena.
Torres Quevedo es uno de los grandes inventores del siglo XX, pero que tristemente hoy, es muy poco conocido
En el museo podemos encontrar varios de sus inventos más famosos y hacer un breve recorrido histórico por su vida","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID285m3gQE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-5-24"},{"Name":"Alex Soriano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVQDetZwOwRcKNg5bR6pvFZNPkpRe4S5ro24rF87uYDZ9oD4e-9=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Museo increíble con piezas únicas. Se tarda unos 70/ 80 minutos con guía, si no,en 30 está visto,es pequeño.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"El Mago 2.0","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUm105mBqAlVdvN-fu1L2cpjUw1hE0LiT2Sl_AUt21zwtSE-JUT=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pregunta por la cafetería de la facultad y verás el museo justo al lado. Suele estar cerrado por lo que para visitarlo hay que concertar una cita mediante email. Totalmente recomendable y sorprendente. Pequeño pero inolvidable museo donde quedarás impresionado por la inteligencia, trabajo y el desarrollo de la carrera profesional del genial inventor Torres Quevedo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC-3Zbl2gE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-11-11"},{"Name":"Castellar zamorano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZioBhy_uHgfsIlgCCLt87t8-2HMseMFk7FbRebj8FSXHtB2Ms=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es un pequeño museo en la Universidad de Caminos en Ciudad Universitaria de Madrid.
Inventor destacado por inventar el Transbordador en las Cataratas del Niágara , actualmente en uso desde 1916.
También los dirigibles, utilizados en la 1a Guerra Mundial, máquina Ajedrecista autómata y máquinas de cálculo y el Telequino, el primer mando a distancia a gran escala, entre otros","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDGtOrv9wE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2021-11-19"},{"Name":"Javier Pulido Porcel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXlOGHzkxgej9EEi-M_em4uUxnnYYs_jhuv-mBxuduf4FG7QXY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Buen lugar que visitar si tienes un rato libre, conocer la historia de Leonardo Torres Quevedo un ingeniero con inventos inimaginables, para la época, muy chula la maqueta del teleférico, que es el mismo que  cruza las cataratas del Niágara. Y otros inventos como máquinas de cálculo, dirigibles, mandos a distancia,etc. El guía, que es ingeniero de caminos nos dio una muy buena explicación de cada uno de los inventos presentes y nos hizo el recorrido a través de la historia de Torres Quevedo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICv3u2_xAE&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2024-12-11"},{"Name":"Cristina González","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW4zXb0A_Qr-gplRtewimQsjXPYFCg09HcFz3uvUxizKbuMW42l=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Imprescindible... Se encuentra dentro de la ETSI Caminos, Canales y Puertos en la Ciudad Universitaria.

Es un museo que alberga la colección de máquinas e ingenios diseñados y producidos por el ingeniero Leonardo Torres Quevedo. Se conservan maquetas, aparatos, documentos y fotografías de los inventos.

El gran desconocido para la población y es un español que hizo historia y qué mejor que visitar este museo para poder conocerlo y darlo a conocer. El museo ocupa una sala.

Entrada gratuita y es imprescindible concertar cita previa por correo electrónico. La visita guiada es en días laborales (lunes a viernes) a las 12:00h y de 75-80 minutos de duración. Explicado maravillosamente.

Se puede ir con niños pero recomiendo que a partir de unos 14 años. Para todas las edades en adultos. Grupos de 12 personas para realizar la visita. Al estar dentro de la facultad hay baños y cafetería de los que se puede hacer uso.

Como llegar al Museo Torres Quevedo:
Metro: Ciudad Universitaria (l-6) / EMT: F, G, U, 82, 132

Un poco estrecha la sala pero creo que sí se puede acceder con silla de ruedas sencilla. Es mejor comentarlo al solicitar la cita.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDT7LrBCw&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2024-5-23"},{"Name":"Manuel GD","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXCVXdYApfqmq_XQEcWweualu0tj4DbMma8TLTkZgZveuZWirts=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Acudimos varios alumnos de la Academia de Ajedrez Chamberí y socios del Club de Ajedrez La Didáctica. ¡Una visita estupenda y totalmente recomendable para cualquier aficionado al Ajedrez!
Nos han explicado estupendamente el Museo entero, y muy en particular los 2 autómatas ajedrecistas, primeros de la historia y nos han abierto sus tripas para ver detenidamente el modelo final.
La verdad es que nos dan ganas de repetir.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC2xbXwZw&fid=0x0:0xd2f2828f8abc358b"],"When":"2022-4-25"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"PL Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_kjCx3N7Oe5EIg03qgHTSqYbHJ9Dr8h6_hZbfJqZYWeYejkv9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A Nostalgic Trip Through Gaming History
⭐⭐⭐⭐

If you have any interest in gaming, OXO Museum is an absolute must-visit in Madrid. It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane for us!

What we loved:

Hands-on Experience: The biggest plus is that you can actually play almost everything. We saw everything from old Commodore computers and classic Sega machines to Pac-Man and a massive range of retro and modern systems.

Childhood Memories: We spent a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console. It was the perfect way to relive old memories while making new ones.

Educational Context: It’s not just an arcade; there are great historical explanations, descriptions, and exhibits that really give you a sense of the evolution of the industry.

A few things to note:
Price: At €21, it is a tad pricey compared to other museums in the city.

Time Limit: Your visit is limited to two hours, which felt somewhat restrictive. Given how much there is to see and—more importantly—play, you have to keep an eye on the clock!

Overall: Despite the price and time limit, it’s a fantastic, well-curated space. Perfect for families or anyone looking to reconnect with their favorite classic games.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBkStWpxMgrPoRNvWDgtfnw&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"Diego Schwarz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIqpGpnlnPKQWw4-vgrZrUxVnsOcgERKIZ7Z9T8_P5T6tSZqA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Honestly, we weren’t expecting much — and wow, were we wrong! 😄 It turned into an amazing walk down memory lane. We had a fantastic couple of hours playing on my childhood console, reliving old memories and making new ones at the same time. Such a fun, special experience for both my kid and me. Highly recommended!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAedZjQDHFVCjbXap6SINXI&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-4"},{"Name":"Joe Szabo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqlMOLx09MrD42Yu00kBfRybp1LlyD_EY8OUGGMpNkMAlhnys4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"OXO Museo del Videojuego Madrid is a fantastic place for anyone interested in the history of video games. Spread across three floors, the museum showcases every generation of gaming from the 1960s and 70s all the way to the latest PlayStation consoles.

You can try almost everything — from old Commodore computers and classic Sega Machines and Pac-Man games to a wide range of retro and modern systems. There are historical explanations, descriptions, exhibits, and plenty of hands-on gameplay, which is a big plus.

The adult ticket is €21, which I find a bit expensive, and the visit is limited to two hours, which feels somewhat restrictive considering how much there is to see and play.

Overall, I’d give it 4 stars — but for anyone who loves video games, it’s definitely worth a visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDMjCWyiWGSXjNSHlW9acs-&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-18"},{"Name":"Siv Carina Molvik","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWfHfJ-c2ljwqULxk8LJK81CWRBkqoj4OUL8Uduvdie9XnbnCI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Whether you are a younger gamer who loves dropping Tilted Towers and playing LEGO versions of games or an older gamer who remembers PONG fondly like it was yesterday, this place is going to amaze you! Spend a couple of hours walking down memory lane of games and play ALL of them! Interactive museum indeed! Highly recommend!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBCro9zn2JwMcS7TJhlB_dG&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2026-1-26"},{"Name":"Kam Loqueman","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUxC4clvyzC3x-fNmSBYU0Vrhvk05LFQnTDSuN-p7D6-yD4FCzj-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One could argue a tad pricey at 21 Euros but for me well worth it. Brought back so much nostalgia and the fact you could touch and play with 40+ year old tech was brilliant. The arcade was great fun playing games of my youth..for free! The Lego exhibition was a bonus.

I visited on a Saturday and it wasn't too busy at all.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBSR2u9NhpzjJrowg7093ry&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-11-16"},{"Name":"Vivian Martinez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVxe-YsjP5k6BiGnY8-7zA7gI6OHs8j_MTCaODEYXkYCZxE__0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you’re a video game fan, this one’s for you! Have never seen anything like it. 7@ years worth of video game history, you get to play in all (and I mean ALL) the consoles you can think of from 70 years back. Truly a blast from the past! Make sure to make a reservation.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAsPWVPbdWKhFNzLTKmrwIA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-10-7"},{"Name":"Lawana “Mitchi” Walker","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKyssC3nkPp_pZqwmcbuNrVmcnOaoycZTYhTvAsDH-Ec2q8UARRw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Went on a weekday afternoon and there were only a couple other families there.

It is a little expensive BUT they do have a reduced rate for families, & an annual pass that I personally think is worth it. I've seen some reviews that said some of the games didn't work, but I only saw maybe 3 or 4 games/systems that weren't working. The history of videogames part is super cool, & they have a huge selection of gaming systems on display & available to play. The arcade is also super cool, the Japanese games were the most fun for my son. The PlayStation floor was pretty cool, too. The entire place is themed very well, it's clean, in good condition & a nice place to spend a couple of hours as a family.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBmtrAubrN2mFcA5v0O8vhQ&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-7-3"},{"Name":"Diogo Morais","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLLo3-eNubWf7buJzmV4e2jyPV9FE1UJME0WrCQxukl-eqvWQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Very good museum with a lot of games and arcades to try out!
My biggest critic is that the parents don't understand their kids can't be on the same game for long time... Needs more control on that part a lot of machines had the same kids there for more than 10 minutes....","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAshlO6DoTDkUTtdUNcLoLA&fid=0x0:0xa8807abaf25ecc1f"],"When":"2025-9-13"}]

Me ha gustado cómo está diseñado, la distribución de las salas por temas y las explicaciones de los paneles informativos. No es muy grande pero es muy completo. Ideal para pasar con niños un rato didáctico.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Manuel Gutierrez Hernandez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXBhNgNShCgkXUUDaMsWqDfvucTT-e5pjyDFfDEBzL13Aw_47pu=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy interesante, el staff muy amable, pequeño pero merece la pena","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhATsOKMdqiOvYznF-9XpxuY&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-7"},{"Name":"Lucía Fernández","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWo-7rJUvFax_U_UfEomhs-QAw3KDdofD-gwXZ6CPawE0ORnTuGVw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El museo de Leonardo Da Vinci está muy bien, sobre todo cuando te lo explican. Las dos personas que nos hicieron la visita lo hicieron genial y así se disfruta mucho más.

Hay maquetas de máquinas e inventos en sus cuadernos de madera hechas a partir de los dibujos de Leonardo y también reproducciones de la Mona Lisa y La Última Cena.

Echo de menos que haya más pantallas o elementos interactivos para entender mejor cómo funcionan las máquinas. Aun así, la visita merece la pena.

Por otro lado pensábamos con tanto vinilo que estaban en obras, porque parecía que estaba cerrado y al abrir la puerta no vimos la taquilla.
Hay descuentos para niños y mayores.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC6JgZvhlyijm5ZQpihWU4U&fid=0x0:0xbfa93202fc6d3d55"],"When":"2026-3-6"}]

title: Museo Ciencias Naturales
address: Chamartín, 28046 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Maxim BK","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXhjS8BtCwS0iOd3gnZ1TwfD3D2_kseC3i6FY8AXKrHZx2JMIZFUQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Visité el Museo de Ciencias Naturales un fin de semana sin demasiadas expectativas y, en general, la experiencia fue positiva. Es un museo cuidado, con exposiciones claras y bien explicadas, especialmente pensadas para un público familiar. Se nota bastante ese enfoque: muchos paneles didácticos, maquetas grandes y zonas pensadas para niños, lo cual está bien si vas con críos, aunque si buscas algo más profundo o especializado puede quedarse un poco corto. Aun así, hay salas interesantes, como la de biodiversidad y fósiles, donde me entretuve más de lo que esperaba. El edificio es amplio y se recorre con calma, sin sensación de agobio. En mi caso fui entre semana y estaba tranquilo, lo que se agradece. No es un museo que te vuele la cabeza, pero sí un plan agradable para pasar un par de horas aprendiendo algo y desconectar del ruido de la ciudad.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"A O","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXMiIq1Xyp9MqeBs443BWLry63RuqPpQMWYDVUjWQj4PBmqtBND=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Hemos ido al Museo de Ciencias Naturales a ver la exposición de Juan Ramón Martín titulada Kranion, este escultor hace un estudio de los cráneos de diversas especies animales en unas bellas composiciones de hierro. Su obra es impresionante , refleja el alma de las especies con gran sencillez de formas. Os recomiendo que visitéis la exposición que estará hasta finales de Julio.
Contigua hay otra exposición “Dibujando entre leones”, con unos bonitos dibujos de estos felinos .
Aprovechamos para visitar el museo , con piezas de principios del Siglo XX algunas de las cuales podían haberse renovado ya que han perdido el color, si bien es cierto que se pueden contemplar gran variedad de especies de nuestro país","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID47KH4xgE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2019-5-12"},{"Name":"Fernando R.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW3g4SyvCMQRxYY4RhtzYhZwQ0c-BUdBDreKXSNolhkr2uR6xDt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Muy recomendable. No es quizá demasiado grande pero tiene una exposición muy representativa y es muy didáctico.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKELWirfenibLRhgE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2025-6-8"},{"Name":"Angel Lillo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUdLxuJxr1HCkfLs-WOngjoMyZ2MA5-cnUl3XIBXwIrvtzojynBCg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Museo pequeño y bien conservado. Es bastante molesto coincidir con varios grupos de colegios sobre todo cuando son adolescentes en plena edad del pavo. Hay una pequeña zona azul para aparcar a cada lado pero mejor ir en transporte público","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC4usfb_QE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2018-3-20"},{"Name":"Teresa PGT","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJAdkENhJmyxCNtvBYf5PrBPf3Ywp5vXFRzf_2Qwh5tq6lBZg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El pintacaras de dinosaurios estuvo bien para acercar el museo a los niños. Sitio interesante","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dolly Bals","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUaWDFzicOIsNv3JZxewTqNtBAFIqU-FC1QxQE_w8CfbL9AtIM=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Fuimos con mucha ilusión al Museo de Ciencias Naturales de Madrid, pero la experiencia fue decepcionante. El museo cierra a las 15:00 y no es imaginable para un turista con niños este horario que no figura en Google, no se puede aprovechar bien la visita si llegas cerca del cierre. Solo pudimos estar 40 minutos.

Además, el museo tiene dos entradas separadas por unos 100 metros, y solo en la más pequeña se pueden sacar entradas, algo confuso si llegas sin saberlo. El personal solo parecía preocupado por que saliéramos cuanto antes. No hubo ni un mínimo de amabilidad o interés en facilitar la visita. Daba la sensación de que estaban deseando terminar su jornada y poco les importaba la experiencia de los visitantes, que hemos pagado una entrada completa como si fuéramos a disfrutar del horario y museo completo.

Una pena, porque el contenido del museo es muy interesante, pero la gestión y el trato al visitante dejan mucho que desear. Deberían revisar sus horarios, su política de entradas y sobre todo, la actitud del personal de cara al público.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"juanjo ovied","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIyqNL2004eo6S_Bi4f1cyNyPutXmkp11yuhI0xcWRUSAjdw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Parada cercana al Museo de Ciencias Naturales, ETS de ingenieros industriales,que comparten manzana con El Laboratorio de electrotecnia, la Residencia de Estudiantes, el Ramiro de Maeztu, y Educación de la CAM,  también està Nuevos Ministerios, iglesia de San Juan de la Cruz.¡Ahh! Se me olvidaba, el monumento del pueblo de Madrid a la Constitución, habitualmente con estudiantes sentados.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgID40uXIDg&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2017-7-9"},{"Name":"Marta FG","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX-00NmE3AnJC7z8tsktVkxGNg6TXDeLdY7eHvO0NFyjRbpxfxI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Hemos hecho la visita con peques y ha estado genial, les llamaba la atención todo y les ha gustado mucho. Es un museo relativamente pequeño dividido en dos edificios, no se hace pesado de visitar. Muy visual e ilustrativo. Buena actividad para hacer con niños!!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICcipjs6gE&fid=0x0:0x8e56161542dccdbb"],"When":"2019-12-1"}]

user_reviews: [{"Name":"Lorenzo Berardinelli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLQYZfs7aGmPhWV6GArevvBQrV3lL4dmCAQU_1872nRZD-NgA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I visited the Museum of Illusions in Madrid with my girlfriend, and it was a fun experience overall. However, I think it’s more enjoyable if you go in a group of more than two people, since many of the photo spots are designed for pairs or groups. Inside, there aren’t that many interactive activities, but the main issue is definitely the number of children running around — though of course, you can’t really ask them not to come! Other than that, we had a good time. I’d recommend going with friends or people you know you can have fun with anywhere.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDmml8-lSdI7MJDcz6dCc1F\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-10-27"},{"Name":"Darren Beck","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLRfTQ5lzSivZGEcb4EWCur2uNoffJUe10rBNwzOdNobY-mUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A little small but worth an hour or so if you want a break from the heat. Note that it definitely skews more toward a younger crowd, and there were a lot of families with little kids there. As a group of two adults though, it was still a fun experience. I’m sure the kids had a great time as well. Staff were great also. Give it a try if you need a break from the sightseeing and just want some fun relaxation and air conditioning!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCDZBKUvmAhb_LOAn8QK7LQ\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-13"},{"Name":"rated by maryam • food critic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV2rFU889_uyIosz_7bpffSFpmpz0-gKcaVKqZvHb7h1qD_qhXV=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Absolutely entertaining from start to finish! The Museum of Illusions in Madrid kept us all fully engaged — we could’ve easily spent two hours exploring. The installations are clever, interactive, and truly high quality. My kids had a blast, and honestly, so did I. Every room is picture perfect, and the staff made sure the flow was easy and fun. Whether you’re going with kids or just want to play with perspective and be wowed, this spot delivers.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCvIn2cbOwD1xlT2Bju8D5l\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-1"},{"Name":"Alex S","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWuqMxZHWW4Q3oWg1MozJ5EDSP48AY-vf9ImsmDzcUsLKEKjbGa=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wow! What a great museum! If you want to find where the magic is at in Madrid, look no further! Offering many illusions for you to try out and capture, this museum is incredibly fun for everyone of all ages except people who otherwise have vertigo, as some of the illusions you will have to walk through may not be good for people with vertigo.\nIf you are wanting a fun activity to do with your kids, it’s a great place! Depending on your pace, you could be at this museum for a while. My favourite illusion was the vortex, as it is a really cool experience and it really makes you feel like you are spinning in an actual vortex. If that’s your thing, it is the last illusion downstairs at the very end of the room. This museum is also not far from the train line, so you can travel here easily.\nIn sum, if you’re wanting something to do while you’re in Madrid, come here!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAiCzHJZBmXN2Y3VDQxPYol\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-7-7"},{"Name":"Michael Stockdale","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIIrWLzicQpgC3hAFUv5MqBQNE3eX1_sKxt7Pa3MR8iF60jyQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"It was okay but l was hoping to see more than l did for the entry price.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCoNl4gi2TxojBRMWk7xy-N\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-12-15"},{"Name":"Rebecca Sirois","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVZzU3H_Gmj---yW_cc8WSqFd5G5oCZ4-hT-20m9bPEiVF3i3R=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Such a fun experience with the kids. Definitely buy tickets in advance, they don't let you in early so show up right on time. Staff were helpful for picture taking otherwise a fun place to wander through at your leisure.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCS7-Y2SbFgXGvCL3e_1CBg\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"Joel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWl5821tyzJrwoJ9WtZ4ckHFtm77vetb8svyUaCTvs_yjbDKrzH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had such a great time at the Museum of Illusions in Madrid! I went with my two kids and they absolutely loved it — from the mind-bending exhibits to all the fun photo ops, there was never a dull moment. A big shoutout to Roberto, who went above and beyond. He was incredibly kind and patient, explaining how the illusions worked in a way my kids could understand, and even helped us take some amazing photos. He showed us the best angles and made sure we got the most out of each exhibit. Definitely recommend for a fun, family-friendly outing. So much fun and lots of laughs!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhA41UAbda0q4GHtxLgczpgq\u0026fid=0x0:0x4b2bd5a4ff4a7207"],"When":"2025-6-29"},{"Name":"David Sargent","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNd7Vx1pxVZ2hd91K_Xk_NBWo4ZUDgz8hEItPBONm_A7r1mRme=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love finding unusual museums, and I read about this one on WikiTravel when I had some spare time on a recwnt business trip. This museum was an absolute delight! My only regret was that I was alone, as a lot of the displays are interactive and the couples and groups were having so much fun watching and photographing each other and srading places and such that I decided that someday I need to go back there with at least one other person who hasn't seen it before. Fingers crossed for another trip to Madrid one of these days!!","Images":null,"When":""}]

[{"Name":"M. W.","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUvsIePFwUvlALEzlUfbwvD0iRDuUSiG02yNj6JVLt16yUCVbCt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The world cultural heritage site lives up to its reputation. Entry requires passport scanning, and the helpful staff ensure a smooth visit. The thoughtful layout makes exploration enjoyable. The reasonable ticket price includes multiple palaces and museums. Book tickets in advance. The scenic area’s clean restrooms are impressive.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBgMEdBrxkCPDZqUS_ZqcRA&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2026-1-11"},{"Name":"Jeanette Lau","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU_sJOzPCiwnhOlZSJUNgV3s6PYZyQW27yclPQY8rYGRT7VPnKn=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Staff was very friendly and courteous especially with clueless Americans eating near priceless monuments.  Lots of historical value. If you enjoy historical architecture,  this is the place you want to visit.  Easy to get to via city bus c30 or c32 or taxi. Note. Buy your tickets Early!!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhACHuDeB66tAD7nbZOQ7jlR&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Onbarefeet","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUVtg_pYjZ_KkdlBI_BCR2iVfAmqark0cKvb0BoLl3fpgUpTwd6=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A Must-See Gem: Alhambra

We booked a 13-hour group tour from Seville to Granada to see the Alhambra, with a stop in the Albaicín, and it was absolutely worth it. The Alhambra’s grounds, palaces, and gardens are breathtaking, showcasing stunning Moorish architecture, intricate tilework, delicate arches, and perfectly manicured courtyards. The combination of geometric patterns, ornate ceilings, and serene water features makes it a visual and historical marvel.

Although the palace doesn’t display personal paraphernalia, what is presented highlights the incredible historical and cultural significance of the architecture, from its defensive structures to its ceremonial halls. Walking through it with a knowledgeable guide adds immense context to the artistry, symbolism, and history embedded in every corner.

This is not a cheap tour, and it is very popular and busy, so booking well in advance with a reputable company is essential. We booked through GetYourGuide (GYG), which allowed quick access to the palace. The coach was clean and comfortable, and we had a tour guide for the journey plus an exclusive local guide for the Alhambra itself, which made the visit much richer.

The tour starts early, so we recommend arranging a breakfast-to-go with your hotel or waiting for the large service station stop halfway to Granada (and again on the return to Seville), as there is no lunch stop included.

Tips:
•	Bring your actual passport for entry.
•	Earplugs for the audio guide can be handy (or pay €1 at the site).
•	Book in advance and ensure your group has a local guide at the Alhambra—it’s invaluable.

A full, immersive day that combines history, architecture, and stunning views—definitely a must-do when visiting southern Spain.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDCd9GoJ3UOOqNys8-7EXiz&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2026-1-10"},{"Name":"Amjad Jan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWwxejYFWSLZqBJY6QJDZx_vXD7GN1BvCC-76g0Uz-QIQ8ZM6OnfQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A Timeless Masterpiece of History and Beauty!

Visiting the Alhambra Palace in Granada was an unforgettable experience. The intricate Islamic architecture, stunning courtyards, and panoramic views of the city and Sierra Nevada mountains made every moment feel like stepping into a living masterpiece. The level of detail in the carvings, tilework, and gardens—especially the Generalife—is simply breathtaking.

Walking through the Nasrid Palaces felt like a journey through centuries of art, culture, and power. The harmony between architecture and nature is unlike anything I’ve seen. Whether you’re a history lover, an art enthusiast, or simply a curious traveler, the Alhambra is a must-see destination that leaves a lasting impression. Truly one of the world’s most beautiful and important heritage sites.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCNWVSbL7Gk0mYllxfB5PIi&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2026-2-8"},{"Name":"Alghazali Taha","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUa0HNQLbDSAsbHDs2S9YuprjAezLTZ8-uVmsz1wDkYy1tnz6M0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Visiting the Alhambra is an unforgettable experience. The architecture and intricate details are absolutely breathtaking, from the palaces to the gardens—the Moorish craftsmanship is stunning.
Walking through the Nasrid Palaces and the Generalife gardens feels like stepping back in time. The views of Granada and the surrounding mountains are incredible, and there are plenty of spots to take in the scenery and snap photos.
It’s best to book tickets in advance because it gets busy, but it’s totally worth the planning. Whether you’re into history, architecture, or just beautiful places, the Alhambra is a must-visit.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhC0AqrSca9chGm_KrFwqpNh&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2025-12-27"},{"Name":"I Berezner","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVpuR88LJbO2mGM0lgRoSSgPom1qNaDTbZc_75JhdnFpfi3w3af7A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Alhambra in Granada is an extraordinary complex that combines palace, fortress, and gardens into one unforgettable visit. Its Nasrid palaces are filled with delicate stucco, intricate tilework, and tranquil courtyards that feel almost otherworldly. The famous patios with reflecting pools and slender columns create a serene, almost dreamlike atmosphere, especially in softer morning or late-afternoon light. The Generalife gardens, with their fountains, flowers, and terraced walks, offer beautiful views back toward the palace and over the city. The site is large and involves a fair bit of walking and stairs, but every section reveals new perspectives and details. Timed tickets and advance booking are essential, and arriving early makes the experience far more relaxed. Overall, the Alhambra fully deserves its reputation: visually stunning, rich in history, and absolutely unmissable for anyone visiting Granada or Andalusia.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAX6lBsCsKaDuRt3vHpXM-g&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"Chuck Powell","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXOsm4HOBCXO2UoHLmHLRsxFs4FUWlp3GQ4QrFkuM_pE1UrAWs=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Beautiful place. Buy your tickets early. The tickets were about 50 bucks for the main part of the castle grounds with a tour guide. 50 extra dollars to go to the fancy area. Its worth it. The tour took about 2 hours and another 5 to walk around on your own and see the rest of it. The tickets sold out by the time we got there at 0900. I bought them three days before and most of the different languages were already sold out. The tour guide said that the busy season can be sold out for up to two months. Its a cool place to visit and even more to see in the city below.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCJ7568cshcs6nRGUw8j7F_&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2026-1-26"},{"Name":"Agatka Hohatka","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVjTVQ9owFUp_GhKPbxHztp3Oz3IXNZFqehYTH98-jA6aKXdXvN=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Alhambra is absolutely breathtaking. The Nasrid Palaces are incredible and fully worth the ticket price, this is something you simply have to see. The entire complex is beautiful, with gardens, views and architecture that keep impressing you. You can easily spend the whole day there. Truly a once in a lifetime experience and a must see in Granada.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCCpLu5mTX_iSUzgBL38k57&fid=0x0:0x808dd1ef1221a27f"],"When":"2026-2-9"}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Corentin Miralles","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLt_-VazHSKUo_2-5mDM34evtHI2aMWlDUtsW4t9_w7CRJz8A=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Fantastic library. Calm, well-maintained, and even architecturally interesting. A big thanks to the Spanish public authorities for making it accessible to everyone for studying.
I can’t help but find it amusing when people complain about the staff not speaking English... Welcome to Madrid, where the official language is...surprise...Spanish! And actually, the person at the front desk speaks French and is incredibly welcoming.
The facilities are well-kept, and in my experience, the bathrooms are always spotless. The only small downside? It would be nice if bringing a bottle of water were allowed.
Overall, a great place to study!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"jake cole","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVgS9W49NTh9eoJbo6EqzKGkRH1D0eUR7ju6RF45RTvU8JReZaH=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"The registration process is kind of annoying, and they don’t let you bring water inside. It’s a pretty library and a nice place to study otherwise, but maybe not worth the hassle. The security guard is a great guy.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lukas Laarmann","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVjwPgFP8D7z_MNz-UZFBUHA8Dh3ykuU4p0QghcLSdcEK_CioGA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"What a sad finding. A super nice and lovely library. Nice to see. Unfortunately, the stuff at the entrance was unfriendly, register process was complex and you are not allowed take anything inside, also no water. Research/Thinking without drinking? How should this work out? Hope to see this changing soon.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Lav Kresovic","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXFJNc1nsVFLsZ8H0Lctj9dx5mL8hICyRqEv0Dkwk_6GwC13noB=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Good library, unbearably rude and unhelpful woman working at the counter","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Vladyslav Deineka","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWQRnKSFIHzIoUtJJYBQFVeyidPcavrJMeALqKIjMxKCQo91pBF=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"One of the best libraries in Madrid! Probably the most remarkable thing is that there are catalogs and analytical reviews of exhibitions, so I found an analytical review of 150 pages, exhibitions of the 90s and the target group that visited this exhibition.

If you have some time to find specialized literature on artists, their lives and work, this is exactly the place you need.

Below I saw reviews that there are rules, yes, I recommend taking one coin for the locker, because the administrator may not have them and yes, you may have to wait for some time in the lobby, but it’s really worth it if you know that you are interested in art.

See you! :)","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"a FM","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKoLGrKtgUY-XcsHcBHp94gdhDR8y3ExLvwXA-39cFERj6H-Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"First, I would like to say thank you for offering us a space to study. But I believe there is some aspects that needs to improve:

- If someone leaves a desk and a new person wants to come in, they won't allow you to get inside for "covid reasons". Even if the library is empty by 5pm, they won't let anyone inside because the desks were not cleaned.

- The security scans the backpack. But we are not allowed to bring the bagpack to the desks. We need to care everything in our hands. Why can't we just bring the bagpack if it was already scanned by security?

- They don't allow us to drink water in the study area. We have to walk 50m to go to our lockers to be able to have a sip and come back to the desk. It's just water.....

- there is a "research floor" where most of the time has empty desks. It would be good if people who need to study could have access to it.

- closing time between 2 and 3pm - first time I see this happening.

Again, it's great to have a space where we can study in peace. But it would be great to have improvment in these topics.

kind regards,","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Davide Iafrate","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWw6ymr2E6ZxptWLRl2KJAa4Ms-SrjQrlUbYVdKbGKIbElrSoWbvA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Very nice library to study, quiet and roomy.
You need to register to get the library card, which can be done at the entrance.
Bring a 1€ or 50c coin to use the lockers as you need to leave your bag there and only bring in the study room what you need to study (laptop, books,…).
There are Wi-Fi and electric plugs.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Xanthe Banham","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ3H7ws394qZVHPFTAiliqBDN8E7zuD7s3DKWYN5L-5Me1v5Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I love this place. Very tranquil place to study on a Friday afternoon and the security guard was super friendly and helpful when I was having trouble with my registration. Yes you have to leave food drink outside but it is easy to understand the reasons for this.","Images":null,"When":""}]

descriptions: Contemporary arts center with regular exhibitions & educational workshops, plus a research library.
owner: {"id":"103497908594141953867","name":"2nd of May Art Center CA2M Museum (Owner)","link":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/103497908594141953867"}
user_reviews: [{"Name":"Geray Mena","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXDii25ExI66-GAWWuYbdcdcPesonptY5aecLAfgyQVGwvKLUiS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"CA2M remains a solid, thoughtfully conceived institution whose programme continues to feel refreshing within the Madrid context and, even more so, in Móstoles. The space itself is correct and functional, allowing exhibitions to unfold with clarity rather than spectacle, and giving real room to artists’ discourses. The current show by Lucía C. Pino is fully recommended: a precise, physically engaging project whose conceptual and material concerns resonate closely with the curatorial line that has been developed at CA2M since the tenure of Manuel Segade. It feels less like an isolated event and more like a natural chapter in an ongoing institutional narrative.\n\nA further reason to visit is the terrace, which is spectacular at sunset. The ideal time, especially in winter, is around 5 p.m. on a weekend, when you can digest the exhibitions while enjoying the fading light and the fresh air.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile\u0026image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAXg_6Wlsf-EtrTcNB2KVrf\u0026fid=0x0:0x7dd6105065222e1e"],"When":"2025-11-23"},{"Name":"Isabel Howlett","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXuZYwdGUgzMr1xTQgenhGjD_GcMgzIlDgcjQ7K-BvqBnKPDGVR=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Great find in Móstoles. The waitress was beautiful and kind.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDzorewCA&fid=0x0:0xef002748979040ce"],"When":"2024-6-8"},{"Name":"Roxana Chiritoiu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVjxa2Ltj28oUOjV6Rq7Aa96YvrLb_5ljfnO6bX8AmefG4nMo2-=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"I recently visited Museo del Jamon in Madrid and overall, had a decent experience. The selection of cured meats was impressive. The prices were reasonable for the quality and quantity of food served. However, the highlight for me was the freshly-sliced ham and other cured meats, which were delicious and high-quality. Despite this, I did have a few minor issues with the service, which detracted from my overall experience. Overall, I would recommend Museo del Jamon for its delicious cured meats, but with the expectation of a slightly less polished dining experience.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICh9t34_QE&fid=0x0:0xef002748979040ce"],"When":"2023-2-12"},{"Name":"Mahboubeh Faghih Mohammadi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUG0b2i1N7lbpjyLCtzUeaQWn_Zxg637qnpxJ03LvpohXi4FFXq=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Definitely recommend it.
Quality and price worth it.
For daily menu, you paid 14€.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMDgn66mHA&fid=0x0:0xef002748979040ce"],"When":"2025-2-28"},{"Name":"小代溝Littledaigo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZ9yLraoJVLJIp1PRLbh4LcdEmAFCBWn5s6JiBmvSnKR_vgUo=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"On of the best meal I had in Madrid. Fresh juice, good coffee latte, yummy ham and bread. It only takes you around 4 euro for this combination. What can I say.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDBvvzpVw&fid=0x0:0xef002748979040ce"],"When":"2023-1-20"},{"Name":"Wan Ping Lee","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWHPbAiYNqCptGHAg14usYBe_yqSFa4XMOdn-PmXQvGGbPfLm__=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Two beers one big plate of Jamon and a small generous plate of Chorizo for 6€. What more can you ask for?! Simple place, great atmosphere, no frills. Highly recommended.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICXqJyxrwE&fid=0x0:0xef002748979040ce"],"When":"2024-10-12"},{"Name":"Ivan T","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV4ydnsk7Wrb7J457HeRuVXsIM8PChM4BaC5CSriNPxHf1M8fqwLg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We came in at around 10am just before it got super busy.

This is a standard Madrid ham coffee shop with a display case at one end and a place to eat and drink at the back.

The display case had many varieties of cheese, hams etc. Hanging above were whole legs of salted hams.

Our food and drinks came quickly. There was a little wait for the plate of Serrano ham but only bordering on 'have they forgotten us or is it because they are busy?' scenario.

This is a great place to take a break, enjoy a snack and then move on. It's also in a good location with metro and bus stops close by and the art museum just over the road.

Wine and beer also available. The menu has pictures and is in Spanish and English.

For two coffees, two ham and cheese croissants and a plate of Serrano ham, the bill came to below €10.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICErKam8gE&fid=0x0:0xef002748979040ce"],"When":"2018-4-14"}]

title: Museo Sorolla
address: Chamberí, 28010 Madrid
user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Jose Garcia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVZ3giLPz2ziBWCFCbpRI3YaT88dz_l2MtIJzKAAnNPitSP6oCwaA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"This beautiful place is run with an absolute ignorance to the customer needing special assistance.
When we asked if wheelchair accessible they excused this necessity by "It is stated in our website we don't "
The elderly and immobile have to walk long distances climb steps and are barred from the second floor.
If you are a costumer needing special assistance this museum discriminates against you.
On top of that the staff is rude to begin with.
Be careful  Museo Sorolla the world is reading this in this part of the web.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Nuan Masa Eros","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLqeUSl3WORL5a3CMEboXX3uWCC__p1bBDzJqnXL5DMr8s_kg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El Museo Sorolla es un tesoro escondido en el barrio de Chamberí que conserva la esencia del gran pintor valenciano Joaquín Sorolla. Este museo-casa permite conocer tanto la vida como la obra del artista en un entorno íntimo y auténtico. El edificio mantiene la distribución original con el taller del pintor, sus salones y un precioso jardín andaluz diseñado por el propio Sorolla, lleno de fuentes, azulejos y vegetación mediterránea. La colección permanente incluye numerosos óleos que muestran su dominio magistral de la luz y el color, especialmente en sus famosas escenas de playa. El museo no está masificado, lo que permite disfrutar de las obras con tranquilidad. La entrada tiene un precio muy accesible. Es una visita imprescindible para los amantes del impresionismo español y el arte decimonónico.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Francisco Javier Toribio Dominguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIQOaCV23hOJZyXCwvLI2zjGRUGwGI6p2RNfRO2lHV6TycI8A=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pequeño, pero gran museo con gran multitud de obras del genial artista, y en estos momentos y por poco tiempo mas con una colección de trajes y complementos repartidos entre este museo y el thyssen muy interesantes y con un patio en pleno centro de Madrid que nos lleva a tiempos pasados.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICE7Y-bEg&fid=0x0:0x493b2c9b15e9fd5e"],"When":"2018-5-21"},{"Name":"Ulricus Angelus","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW-TXBol6ahJS4nIPaNJPPjeTbb1l9alGTE43IXPPHSeqc8qPvS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Wunderschönes Museum in eines kleinen schattigen Park umgeben von vielen höheren Häusern. Wohnhaus, Arbeitsplatz und Galerie des großen Malers. Unbedingt empfehlenswert.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC4j7nHcQ&fid=0x0:0x493b2c9b15e9fd5e"],"When":"2017-6-4"},{"Name":"Gustavo Rodríguez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIYJpJyxyNyb2zy1jw4SUD82YE9x52J5hNWgrbkpnZ58B2R9tQB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un museo muy agradable, un pequeño paraíso para tomarse un respiro. El jardín es bonito y tranquilo. El mayor encanto del museo es que era la casa del pintor y su taller, por lo que es un paseo por las diferentes estancias de la casa. Destaca el taller del pintor, muy luminoso y de techos altos, con cuadros con cuadros colgados por toda la estancia.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Juan Jose Rodriguez Vela","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUd3nPueiu3obk8l3hU8aDGR_ZjQ5r09RG45HTxuD-pKG23c1qZng=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un rincón de lujo en Madrid. Aparte de la gozada de las obras de Sorolla, la casa en sí misma es una maravilla. Un descanso entre un paisaje urbano agobiante.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Isidoro Galán Muñoz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUwwYOQkO46k5xVNnHbwDGzq4Xc3wMi6I-amS4fx0K1jhJ4LDjx=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Un precioso museo ubicado en la calle del general Martinez Campos, un palacete que servia de vivienda y taller fel pintor Joaquín Sorolla.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Dolores Garcia Salado","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKCA-_GdV8zbPmre5aqK8U77UJcMWIQob1P05XLu-4wMYcGDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nos ha encantado la visita...Es maravilloso que la viuda e hijos de Sorolla quisiera ofrecer un trozo de su vida a tantas personas del mundo que tenemos la suerte de compartir.","Images":null,"When":""}]

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"patidan LL","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWj8MPGZZCPJXP3BPu2sVc04EsJ9l_LqjjCXwHTne812fpvztK-=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you want to try a good and cheap place in the city center of Madrid this Museum. The food was very good, the Jamón Ibérico and two beers for €7.90 wooow, we were flying from Dublin so was so cheap. The patata and chorizo definitely the best option. If you want to try something nice I recommend Tinto de Verano, delicioso.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCEjNJB4Xr6dbcss4ZGjIQD&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2026-2-5"},{"Name":"Mariana Oliveira","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX73Yh-irgKVSwfDJ4SNW0h4dEvEUtKcELd2n3AgA1wQS9Cftb9Kg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"The food quality was very poor: the calamari tasted worse than supermarket ones, the tortilla wasn't good, and the ham croquettes barely had any ham. The patatas bravas were the worst I’ve ever had. On top of that, they charged an unexplained "tourist tax." I tried to speak in Spanish, but the waiter had no patience and ended up shouting at me to make himself understood. Wouldn't recommend this place.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBi0BpxFbtps1Mlx_WolM7b&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2026-2-17"},{"Name":"Andrej Sinkovič","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXzjSZv9-mzVHtQ1b9F9wShjdTmRBHaUpEgHaKNwP2kncuWMolnJw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"We visited Museo del Jamón on Plaza Mayor as a pre-booked group of 17 people, and the experience was disappointing from start to finish.

If you’re looking for unprofessional and unpleasant service, terrible food, and a chaotic atmosphere, this is the place for you.

The “veal steak” (see attached photo) was literally 3mm thick, cold, flavorless, and as unappealing as the overall experience. What was meant to be a fun group dinner turned into a frustrating evening that none of us enjoyed.

Save yourself the time and money — skip this place. There are far better options in Madrid.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhBewjVVWgL5BejrLhBb-ckZ&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2025-10-22"},{"Name":"Paul Hill","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL8vSYcQorsyV7t_rbU9dOmUFPK4AmiXEPpKT5EsNCGnIQl-g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Oh dear..Expected alot from this place, unfortunately it did not meet expectations. Sat on the terrace in Plaza Mayor, good location and atmosphere. Staff were ok, gave us english menus(we could have managed the spanish ones)but not wine list..found that on the website. There was a communication error with the starter as my husband wanted a starter,I did not. I ordered the Iberian ham, hubby..the steak. I have to admit the disappointment on receiving my plate..considering it was 29€ There was a substantial amount of tomato and peppers, the pork looked grey and poorly cooked. I returned it and they brought another, more colour this time but raw inside. My husband during this time ate alone. The lady serving us, tried very hard but at this point I just wanted to leave.
Just to note prices are more expensive in the evening.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCaCYhui68ASNK_A-2X88Oy&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2025-9-7"},{"Name":"Nina Kim","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIwPL2__xFWBSlsc4JBnXriV4xL-DXDptpD4ucOfKwji44B3Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Wanted to try jamon while being in Madrid and were still full from dinner so only ordered jamon.
Actually thought the 75% tasted better than the 100%.
Just ok, could not tell if it was exceptionally better than other places.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCRUNekbnKseuRo5TcUcwaw&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"Amrish Joseph","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL5_v5xAq-Gmj2dd4feLYHViSTbAnrXLLWVa83w1dQjmtqi-OUh=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Stopped in for dinner of Tapas while at the Plaza.
The atmosphere was good with comfortable vibes. However the food was not worth the visit. The prize winning croquettes was literally the worst I have had this entire trip. They were tiny stodgy balls with absolutely no Jamin in them. They should be ashamed to call them croquettes.
The chorizo in cider was fair but the Jamon tasting selection was underwhelming. It was dry and tasteless.
The black pudding lacked any character and the peppers were overdone.
This is one of those tourist trap restaurants that everyone warns you about.
I would give it a miss","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCwt6OcTg&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2025-3-21"},{"Name":"Clo","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJI7Dkut86zLQW-rhBIwrcWFX9sfE-aXbJI3BK9GNdyNfJnlr4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Ate here as it was the nearest and we were really hungry and couldn’t be bothered to look for another place.

We ordered jamon serrano, mixed paella, and calamari. We only liked the jamon, but even that was just average. The paella lacked flavor and I think they just reheated it because it came out pretty quick. The calamari was dry and bland. Generous serving for all though.

We came during lunch time, so understandably the staff was busy, but they felt standoffish when they finally got to us.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICbuc_cJw&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2024-7-29"},{"Name":"John Moore","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXaWES2YyoitavpbzowJi0zodecG2XeyrX-b6QDPclZr8ilioGe=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Decided to walk to this restaurant  for lunch since our room was not ready at the RIU Plaza Espana.

A great place to sample Jamon Serrano Ham as well as other items such as Croquettes,sandwiches and local beer.

There are counters in front for informal dining  as well as tables to enjoy a sit down meal. Many locals  came in to purchase ham to go.

Ham is fantastic and there are rows of hams dangling from the ceiling or lining the walls. A great photo opportunity..lol!

For a good sample of Spanish/Madrid fare,give them a try.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDng8eknwE&fid=0x0:0x12ff854a7ec19ecb"],"When":"2024-10-9"}]

[{"Name":"Ed Bad","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVbBkf87brRjKt_Gt0B7Vggs-aPgRPIoY5XJvvj-5s1wuGY2B4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"What a fantastic place!
First, we were pleasantly surprised that our visit was approved on an otherwise closed day.
Our assigned guide, Officer Romo, went above and beyond to make our visit a truly memorable experience.
His good command of English and extensive knowledge of the history and technical details of every item in the museum
truly impressed us and was a wonderful surprise.
We could closely examine and even hear and smell some of the tanks, which our guide had awakened.
The Museum also has a nice shop where you can buy apparel, medals, pins, and other souvenirs.

Again, thank you for the opportunity to learn about this part of the Spanish history!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICP9uCw4QE&fid=0x0:0x7a6e26f397f77218"],"When":"2024-11-24"},{"Name":"Bruno","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJ6QpuGJBsmm8XlV_a5xNNyR5YP1r0PWvxsXZJ39g1fq8Fldg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Quite a good museum. The guided tour is in Spanish and it was thoroughly enjoyable (props to Corporal Luis). You can arrive by bus very easily as well. The entry is free but you have to email the base to reserve a spot","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDLjZqAWA&fid=0x0:0x7a6e26f397f77218"],"When":"2024-6-29"},{"Name":"Eduardo Baracuhy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWy1868yiiGZOarTQjWRM3el5Y7z2WfYmuQCMMevpqz7nIaat8w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Very bad, the soldier in the reception was rude, to visit you should book a day with guided visit, this gave a bad impression to me about the Spanish army","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Niko","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLSZGi1x6rKOXTBkJ6ZG81MFjIR4-oLp-TUIFKTctzfCHLjOw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Excellent collection of soldier uniform and equipment on first part out tour from last hundreds of years, telling story how equipment and uniforms have been developing

Second tour is excellent tour of throughout the last century of tank equipment, ranging from early French/German/Soviet technology to cold war era US/French armor

Remember to sign up with email before your visit","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Capybara Connoisseur","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWkpJer1VxufkdkYjlLRs7Lz-jBr3U-rU5-1se_nN6rwQOiMuaI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Sounds interesting, but only for reservation they won't let you buy ticket on site which is shame.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Edu Palomar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWL2bArCzHifhM_0ekuHo9DFsvaZgNm7AKUwvllETJE3BusMvaG=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Expectacular","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"FormalHusk","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUOq7nUy2UYkuiRiHiIfZyCxUr2RR8OoKhzR-7vxcc-7iifY4jg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba5-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"El guía se ve que le gusta de lo que habla, y aunque aburra un poco y tenga algún error, fue muy simpático y responde a las preguntas. Pero no me gusta que hallan escondido el Leopard 2A4, aunque sea por razones políticas; que muchas piezas estén descuidadas y oxidándose y que hay pocas piezas.
Estuvo guay que había una torreta de un M60 para ver su interior y una ventana para ver el interior del Sonderkraftfahrzeug 161 Panzerkampfwagen IV ausfhürung H. También el guía arrancó el M47 para que escuchemos el motor, fue chachi.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCJggkF8vhmCBzd0k-wU-wG&fid=0x0:0x7a6e26f397f77218"],"When":"2025-12-14"},{"Name":"Jose Prieto","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKWAKZw7Kj9SI69BurVIdBvrpB5Mqcv0eOaOvdfTDvP43Y5GA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Impresionante museo del Ejército. Muy recomendable para aprender más sobre esta rama de las Fuerzas Armadas. A destacar la profesionalidad y cercanía del subteniente que nos explicó con todo lujo de detalle las piezas expuestas, las cuales estaban tratadas con todo el cuidado. Un privilegio haber podido visitarlo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCyEshMXRvAUaQlu9SnbVlV&fid=0x0:0x7a6e26f397f77218"],"When":"2025-11-8"}]

[{"Name":"Georges Younes","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX1af_MnotjZ8yBIdT9rPJYbzM4SW-WSjOmDsOKhwb6iDjgoVaIUg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Claustro Jerónimos (ampliación Museo del Prado) is an impressive modern building that has the most remarkable door as its main entrance.I arrived there by chance after my visit to the adjacent church of San Jerónimo el Real.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDZ5sHDDA&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2023-9-30"},{"Name":"Pablo Arellano","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX57pzezBkUV6igy0-bizi9GVUe2nTsz-HcuWpdjIk0AO9W_CyQyg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The black paintings are culture distilled.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marco Soave","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWbAFsD8tcBhnSrW-_VLjoy-5UP4HlzAU6RMa47gi0KFkXNYk9pKg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Great space for events. Very magical. Used to be the cloister of the cathedral and was used in former times for wedding cocktails and such events, now it is still used to host events but within the context of the Prado Miseum","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Carmen mendez ayala","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL-78cNA2vUKujKoiZ2z7MVlQTCbrf4eZalnfSS868UlhzbVA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Esoectacular","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Gogara Arkitektura","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWY6jDT2RpjbdhXi4TeWiLeDVxwVKV-mYRRzgTqNYRnmupeTKwk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"El claustro del Museo Nacional Centro de Arte Reina Sofía se percibe como un espacio especialmente agradable dentro del recorrido del museo, pensado casi como una pausa necesaria cuando el cansancio empieza a notarse tras varias salas.

Arquitectónicamente, la solución es muy sugerente. El claustro original aparece suspendido, “volando” sobre una estructura de hormigón visto, que se muestra sin intentar mimetizarse. La estructura no pesa visualmente, sino que enmarca y realza el valor del claustro original.

El patio central actúa como verdadero corazón del espacio. La proporción, la luz y la ausencia de ruido lo convierten en un lugar tranquilo, donde el tiempo parece desacelerarse.

La experiencia de calma la percibimos ya que al ser lunes, el claustro estaba prácticamente vacío, lo que acentuó esa sensación de refugio dentro del museo.

Lejos de ser un espacio residual, funciona como un lugar de descanso real, tanto físico como mental, que equilibra muy bien la intensidad del recorrido expositivo.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhDik19UFagP_sYEXD5emUej&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2026-2-20"},{"Name":"Francisco Lucena","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXXAnbPEgO38IAgd5mIrYHf4QXKfnMypv1751f6z9U8cL9E_HQHDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba8-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"La ampliación del claustro de los Jerónimos es una espectacular obra de Rafael moneo en 2007 incluyen la recuperación y rehabilitación del claustro de los Jerónimos que perteneció al antiguo monasterio de Jerónimo el real, utilizando gran parte de su estructura original.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhAaCF038YEMtiB28iO2eG6_&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2025-9-14"},{"Name":"Fernando Anayet","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUioCJNuamwUKIFiD6_N5CG67YAYQLWgVY6ygBcezFzyMDDJnk=s120-c-rp-mo-ba6-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Es parte de la última ampliación del Prado en manos de Moneo.

Es interesante como edificio y alberga las colecciones temporales del Prado, dándole más espacio.

La puerta principal simula la corteza de un árbol.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCYxdCkTg&fid=0x0:0xfd3c86f95e23fa1a"],"When":"2025-5-5"},{"Name":"Etsel Roberto Eschoyez","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKCliESF1ORBDFthhaKlTcq5JX4y_bcC2V-L4G98rx9WNp4Iw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba4-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Me pareció cara la entrada por lo que se puede ver en su interior. Pensábamos que accederiamos a gran parte del monesterio pero no es así. De lo que se puede visitar, muy lindo.","Images":null,"When":""}]
